runners.spotlight: No Risk At All gelding was purchased for 65,000euros as a 3yo; dam placed in French 2m2f hurdle, half-sister to winners Kopek Des Bordes (high-class bumper and 2m-2m1f hurdle/chase), Utopie Des Bordes (useful 2m-3m hurdle/chase) and Darling Des Bordes (useful 2m1f-2m6f hurdle/chase); interesting newcomer, but Ole Ole is stable first-string; declared for 5.30 at Ballinrobe on Friday.
runners.comment: Related to high-class Kopek Des Bordes; interesting newcomer but Donoghue rides Ole Ole

runners.name: Monntie
runners.spotlight: First reserve; some positives in a couple of her Flat runs last year; makes stable/hurdling debut now but others more likely.
runners.comment: Reserve; some positives in Flat runs last year; makes stable/hurdling debut now

runners.name: Pilgrims Walk
runners.spotlight: Always out the back in maiden hurdles at Cork and Limerick; may find his level in handicaps.
runners.comment: Always out the back in both maiden hurdles; may find his level in handicaps

runners.name: Littlebeen
runners.spotlight: Slight promise in her 31l third of six in her Punchestown bumper debut in February (2m1f, soft); fair chunk of improvement needed here.
runners.comment: Slight promise in her bumper debut but fair chunk of improvement needed

runners.name: Finns First
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to 2m/2m4f hurdle winner Iknowwhereiamnow; dam pulled up in points, half-sister to 2m-4m1f hurdle/chase winner Jacks Island and bumper/useful 2m-2m4f hurdle winner Good Thyne Tara; best watched on debut
runners.comment: Half-brother to Iknowwhereiamnow (RPR 121); best watched on debut

runners.name: Free Vango
runners.spotlight: Dam 2m3f hurdle winner, closely related to 2m hurdle winner Getting Ready, half-sister to useful winners Final Assault (2m-3m hurdle/chase) and Last Encounter (2m4f hurdle); unlikely to make an immediate impact.
runners.comment: Dam 2m3f hurdle winner but unlikely to make an immediate impact

runners.name: Medieval Night
runners.spotlight: Winless from six runs on the Flat for Dermot Weld; best of those efforts was when second of 21 at Leopardstown on handicap debut when favourite; that run came after an absence, so perhaps this is the time to catch him on hurdle/stable debut.
runners.comment: Winless from six on Flat but best run came after an absence; makes stable/hurdling debut

runners.name: Asthelightdeclines
runners.spotlight: Near the back and under pressure when brought down at two out on debut at Naas; tailed off at Cork earlier this month; watching brief advised.
runners.comment: BD when near the back on debut and tailed off last time; watching brief advised

runners.name: Betty's Glen
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; tailed off on bumper debut at Punchestown; ruled out.
runners.comment: Reserve; tailed off on bumper debut at Punchestown; ruled out

runners.name: Walk Win
runners.spotlight: Second effort at Cork was better than his first at Thurles but huge improvement required to get involved here.
runners.comment: Second effort at Cork was better the first at Thurles but huge improvement required

runners.name: Codice
runners.spotlight: Showed some ability on the Flat in France last year without winning but has been poor in two Irish runs, including when 34l behind on hurdling debut at Limerick; best watched.
runners.comment: Signs of ability on Flat in France but poor in two Irish runs inc' over hurdles

runners.name: Star Of Kilbride
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; pulled up on debut in a Cork academy hurdle when 150-1 (2m2f, yielding to soft); very unlikely.
runners.comment: Reserve; PU on debut in Cork academy hurdle when 150-1; very unlikely.

runners.name: Westin Pimpernel
runners.spotlight: Huge odds for three maiden hurdle runs and ran like it; passed over.
runners.comment: Huge odds for three maiden hurdle runs and ran like it; passed over

runners.name: Crest Of Light
runners.spotlight: Two-time AW winner has been tailed off at triple-figure odds in both maiden hurdle runs and has to carry a penalty.
runners.comment: Two-time AW winner has been tailed off at triple-figure odds in both maiden hurdle runs

races.race_name: William Hill Each Way Extra Challenge Series Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Grandy Lane
runners.spotlight: One of two for trainer; just denied in a 2m4f maiden hurdle (good) on rules' debut for Ian Donoghue and again on stable debut in a Clonmel bumper in November (soft); hasn't hit the same heights since, although creditable effort in a strong Leopardstown maiden hurdle; won't want ground too soft.
runners.comment: Just denied on hurdle debut and again in a bumper; chance if back at that level
runners.owner: Darren Cahill/JRM Racing Syndicate/Mrs J P Duffy

runners.name: Daisy Fingall
runners.spotlight: Very modest efforts in maidens at Fairyhouse and Leopardstown just under a year apart; much improved to take a 2l second at Limerick last month (2m, soft); interesting with a little more improvement.
runners.comment: Much improved in a Limerick maiden last time when runner-up; interesting

runners.name: Two Pair
runners.spotlight: Promise in both bumpers and posted a career best when runner-up on hurdling debut at Clonmel in October (2m4f, yielding); decent fifth at Fairyhouse next time (2m5f, heavy) but her Limerick run last time was disappointing; chance if back to her best.
runners.comment: Promise in bumpers and opening hurdle runs; chance if bouncing back from poor run latest

runners.name: Sticktothescript
runners.spotlight: Placed in only point-to-point; ran nicely at Punchestown in January (2m4f, soft to heavy) but hasn't replicated that in two runs since, including when a well beaten favourite at Navan last month (2m4f, soft); blinkers go on now.
runners.comment: Placed in sole point; beaten fav' at Navan last month; cheekpieces go on now

runners.name: Following Orders
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden but some good runs in defeat; back-to-back runner-up finishes in maiden hurdles at Roscommon and Ballinrobe last August on better ground; way below that at Downpatrick when last seen in September.
runners.comment: Back-to-back runner-up last Aug' on better ground but way below that when last seen

runners.name: Kasper Hauser
runners.spotlight: Is 0-8 over hurdles; okay run when a 6l sixth of ten in a Cork maiden hurdle in October (2m, good); struggled in Grade 2s at Cheltenham and Leopardstown but not beaten too far on handicap debut at Punchestown (2m4f, heavy); Punchestown run was a step backwards.
runners.comment: 0-8 over hurdles but h'cap debut was not too bad; Punchestown run was a step backwards
runners.owner: One In A Million Syndicate/Trevor Payne/B Sheerin

runners.name: Glenrush Bay
runners.spotlight: One of two for trainer; first sign of ability when a 23l second of 17 in a Thurles maiden hurdle in November; made late headway to take fourth of seven in a novice hurdle at Cork last time (2m1f, yielding to soft); stable first-string and in the mix.
runners.comment: Decent efforts in his last two runs put him in the mix and is stable first-string

runners.name: Zuzukel
runners.spotlight: Frankel gelding was narrowly denied in a Punchestown handicap in November (2m, heavy); weakening when fell at the final flight at Down Royal in a novice hurdle when last seen in January (2m1f, soft); has the form to win a race of this nature.
runners.comment: Frankel gelding was narrowly denied in a h'cap before a faller last time; player

runners.name: Tippawan Haa
runners.spotlight: Showed some promise over further before dropping in trip and posting his best effort at Cork in August when second (2m3f, good); reported to have burst a blood vessel when well beaten at Ballinrobe later that month (2m3f, good); contender if ready and strength in the market would be interesting.
runners.comment: Good second at Cork and excuses for poor run next time; contender if ready

runners.name: Chanceawetmorning
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; 11-race maiden but was second of 12 finishers when a beaten favourite at Fairyhouse (2m4f, soft) in January; didn't build on that when failed to complete at Ayr last month; makes stable debut.
runners.comment: Reserve; maiden was second on penultimate but PU last time; makes stable debut

runners.name: Microscope
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in all four points; fairly moderate hurdle/bumper form as well; others more likely.
runners.comment: Pulled up in all four points; fairly moderate hurdle/bumper form also; others more likely

runners.name: Speedy Box
runners.spotlight: Top-rated of the field; ran a couple of reasonably encouraging races in maidens last season; set to run well at Naas (2m, soft to heavy) in March when fell at two out; poor effort over fences last time; may get involved at his best.
runners.comment: Top-rated of the field was running well when falling at Naas; poor chase run latest

runners.name: Jazz Forever
runners.spotlight: Front-runner is 1-28 under rules (the win coming on AW in 2024) and was disappointing at Dundalk when last seen; 0-5 over hurdles, with his best effort a 14.5l fifth behind subsequent Grade 1 winner Koktail Divin at Leopardstown (2m4f, yielding); probably flattered by that placing, but it's interesting that Jack Kennedy takes the ride (2-5 riding for this trainer).
runners.comment: Overall form is nothing to write home about but interesting that Kennedy rides

runners.name: Double Power
runners.spotlight: First reserve; 0-9 in point-to-points; best of three maiden hurdles came on rules debut at Leopardstown when a 23l sixth of 11 (2m5f, good to yielding).
runners.comment: Reserve; 0-9 in points and moderate maiden hurdle form

runners.name: Lake Muritz
runners.spotlight: February foal; third foal; by Oasis Dream out of 1m/1m1f winner (RPR 69), half-sister to 5.7f/7f winner Bussento (Britain and Bahrain); no sign as yet that the yard's juveniles are forward.
runners.comment: No sign as yet that the yard's juveniles are forward; others look more likely this time

runners.name: Niewiadoma
runners.spotlight: April foal; £40,000 yearling; second foal; by Cotai Glory out of 5f winner (including 2yo; RPR 80), half-sister to 5f Group 2 winner Liberty Beach (also Group 3 winner as 2yo; 110); yard had first 2yo runner of the year finish third at Bath yesterday; market can guide.
runners.comment: April foal; dam 5f-winning half-sister to smart sprinter Liberty Beach; market can guide

races.race_name: Thomas Lord Dine & View Restaurant Great Value Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Inishbeg
runners.spotlight: 500,000gns yearling; runner-up to a smart prospect at Redcar (7f, good to soft) last autumn; faded after leading until over 1f out when second to a wide-margin winner at Newcastle (7f, AW) on reappearance; still has untapped potential; leading candidate.
runners.comment: Second at Redcar (to smart prospect) and Newcastle (behind wide-margin winner); big shout
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Advance Twentyfive
runners.spotlight: 0-7 last year but quite consistent, close fifth at York (7f, good) on final start; subsequently gelded; far more solid than most of these and likely to be in the thick of things after his winter break.
runners.comment: Consistent as 2yo; likely to be in the thick of things after his winter break

runners.name: Zarvali
runners.spotlight: Some promise on first three 2yo starts and he improved when second in nursery at Fairyhouse (6f, yielding; blinkered) in September; subsequently changed hands for 25,000euros and gelded; still low mileage and may progress further for new stable.
runners.comment: Improved when second in nursery on last of four starts in Ireland; still low mileage

runners.name: Blue Boy Junior
runners.spotlight: £80,000 2yo (breeze-ups); by Space Blues; dam placed 7f AW (RPR 74), half-sister to Listed winners Hawke (10.6f) and Mireille (7f); worth a market check on debut.
runners.comment: £80,000 breeze-up purchase last year; worth a market check on debut

runners.name: Clova Royale
runners.spotlight: 3,000gns 2yo; by Territories; closely related to French winners Perfect Stranger (7.5f AW; RPR 87) and Palmares (1m5f); dam French 1m 2yo winner (94); may not come into his own until getting more of a test of stamina.
runners.comment: Newcomer by Territories; may not come into his own until getting more of a test of stamina
runners.owner: Clova Farms And P D Niven

runners.name: Deep Sleep
runners.spotlight: Signs of ability in three runs as 2yo, fading into fourth of ten at Catterick (7f, good) on final one; may have more still to offer but a couple of these have much stronger form.
runners.comment: Signs of ability in three runs as 2yo but a couple of these have much stronger form

runners.name: Dream Odyssey
runners.spotlight: 13,000gns yearling; Dream Ahead half-brother to winners Ayr Harbour (7f/1m AW; RPR 105), Desert Lion (1m-1m2f; 104) and Navajo Scout (Italian 11.5f); dam 7f and 7.5f (Italian Listed) 2yo winner (103); worth monitoring in the betting, although yard isn't known for first-time out winners.
runners.comment: Worth monitoring in the betting, although yard isn't known for first-time out winners

runners.name: Haltonwood
runners.spotlight: Made a low-key start at a big price on debut at Newcastle (1m, AW), and went backwards over the same C&D next time; may be of more interest in handicaps.
runners.comment: Went backwards from debut at Newcastle last time; may be of more interest in handicaps

runners.name: Simba's Pride
runners.spotlight: Off 11 months between debut and reappearance; possibly failed to stay 1m on return and this shorter trip should help but he has plenty to find on the figures.
runners.comment: Off 11 months between debut and reappearance; has plenty to find on the figures

runners.name: Weffaag
runners.spotlight: Slowly away and never the slightest threat at Newmarket (7f, good; 33-1) on sole outing last term; needs a big leap forward but he was an expensive (420,000gns) yearling and he's in good hands so it's possible.
runners.comment: No threat on debut but he was an expensive yearling and he's in good hands

runners.name: Talking In Kode
runners.spotlight: Finished two places and 3l behind Forest Phoenix on debut at Catterick (28-1) ten days ago; should improve but others still make more appeal.
runners.comment: Finished two places and 3l behind Forest Phoenix on debut at Catterick ten days ago

runners.name: Forest Phoenix
runners.spotlight: Gelded son of Lope De Vega; weak in the market and slowly away when over 10l fifth of 11 at Catterick (7f, good; 40-1) ten days ago; made some late headway and should improve, but he needs to.
runners.comment: Some late headway when fifth at Catterick ten days ago; should improve but needs to

runners.name: Fozzie
runners.spotlight: 82,000gns yearling; only seventh of nine on both outings last term, folding tamely after racing freely on second of them; lots of improvement required but he has since been gelded and still has potential.
runners.comment: Only seventh in two runs last year but has been gelded and still has potential

runners.name: Saxon Gem
runners.spotlight: Comes from a family that has served Jim Goldie very well, but minimal promise in two runs last term and probably best watched until handicapping; wears a hood for the first time.
runners.comment: Comes from a family that has served Jim Goldie well, but minimal promise last term

runners.name: Pop The Question
runners.spotlight: 2m4f hurdle winner; well held (7f/1m) on Flat and hard to fancy until tackling handicaps over longer distances.
runners.comment: 2m4f hurdle winner; hard to fancy on Flat until tackling handicaps over longer trips

runners.name: Act Of Honor
runners.spotlight: Poor form in two runs last year for Kevin Ryan; changed hands for 11,000gns in the autumn and gelded; needs this run for a handicap mark and probably best watched unless strong in the market.
runners.comment: Poor form in two runs last year for Kevin Ryan; needs this run for a handicap mark

races.race_name: Nick Simpson Cannot Be 60 Handicap

runners.name: Sherlock
runners.spotlight: Just one win from 22 starts but he is unexposed as a sprinter; apprentice takes off a handy 3lb and he holds each-way claims.
runners.comment: Infrequent winner but unexposed as a sprinter and holds each-way claims

runners.name: Mr Dreamseller
runners.spotlight: He has improved with each of his four starts, getting off the mark in good style at Wolverhampton ten weeks ago (6f); while that isn't the most solid piece of form, he isn't being overfaced for his handicap debut and remains capable of further progress.
runners.comment: Improving run by run, getting off mark in small-field novice (6f) in February; unexposed

runners.name: Knicks
runners.spotlight: Getting on for two years since his last win; on a handy mark if ready to roll after 172 days off and he has run well fresh in the past.
runners.comment: This mark not beyond him and he has run well fresh after a break; betting useful

runners.name: Stirrup Cup
runners.spotlight: Both his wins have come on soft ground but he ran a cracker on good ground over C&D last week (cheekpieces first time), keeping on well behind an in-form sprinter; 1lb higher today but still a strong contender, especially with some rain in the forecast.
runners.comment: Fine 2nd over C&D on last week's return to action; 1lb higher but still looks a key player

runners.name: Ay Gee Ell
runners.spotlight: Disappointing at Doncaster on his return to turf last month (5f, good to soft); in good form on AW beforehand and this mark should be well within his range; not sure what to expect.
runners.comment: Disappointing at Doncaster on his return to turf; should be well treated but not solid

runners.name: Brave Empire
runners.spotlight: 8lb lower than for his Newcastle third in December (6f) but that hasn't been a springboard to better things; hopes rest on the return to turf perking him up.
runners.comment: Should be well handicapped but he has been disappointing so far this year
runners.owner: James Paul Services To Industry Ltd

runners.name: Solar Biricz
runners.spotlight: Raced exclusively at 5f, winning twice for Brian Ellison; starts out for his new stable off a dangerous mark and the return of cheekpieces can help; stable in the winners on Thursday; betting useful.
runners.comment: Two 5f wins for former trainer; cheekpieces return for stable debut

runners.name: Pal Joey
runners.spotlight: Ran on well to win a 5f handicap at Newcastle in January; not quite so good back there six days later but he is feasibly weighted and 6f on good ground no problem; stable operating at a fine strike-rate in 2026.
runners.comment: 5f AW win in January; not so good six days later but can bounce back after a break

runners.name: Yes I'm Mali
runners.spotlight: Won three times as a 2yo for Richard Fahey; ran well for a close third at Newcastle on his final run for that stable last October (6f; visored); low-key stable debut at Doncaster three weeks ago; visor absent once again.
runners.comment: Three wins as a 2yo but drawn a blank since; needs to step up on last month's stable debut

runners.name: Kings Merchant
runners.spotlight: Two of his four wins have come over C&D; signs he was ready to exploit his reduced mark when fourth of nine at Newcastle three weeks ago; not discounted.
runners.comment: Two-time C&D winner; on a good mark and latest Newcastle fourth wasn't without hope

runners.name: Ashen
runners.spotlight: 0-9 but he hasn't been with Jim Goldie for long and getting back on turf should help; drop back to 6f another possible source of improvement.
runners.comment: 0-9 but he hasn't been with Jim Goldie for long; return to turf and 6f could both help

runners.name: Lord Abama
runners.spotlight: His three turf wins have come over C&D and he made a pleasing seasonal return when fifth of 15 here (Stirrup Cup second) eight days ago; one for the shortlist.
runners.comment: Three-time C&D winner; good C&D run eight days ago but work to do with Stirrup Cup
runners.owner: Mrs K S Pratt & M Dods

runners.name: Monotone
runners.spotlight: Dropping down the weights and his latest Pontefract fifth was marginally more encouraging; more required back up in class though.
runners.comment: Latest fifth wasn't without hope but he is back up in class today

runners.name: Taygar
runners.spotlight: On a good mark on last summer's best; she is returning from six months off and she has been unplaced on her reappearance in the last two seasons.
runners.comment: On a good mark on last summer's best; returns from six months off; betting to guide

runners.name: Iris Dancer
runners.spotlight: Best known for her Hamilton exploits but she is effective elsewhere and she ran well on her seasonal return 11 days ago (5f, good to soft; ground-saving ride against the rail), keeping on from well back to take second (apprentice jockey's first ride under rules); no surprise to see her in the shake up.
runners.comment: Enjoyed ground-saving ride when 2nd at Pontefract on her return 11 days ago; respected

runners.name: One More Dream
runners.spotlight: The winner of 13 races, but just three of them have been on turf; yet to shine this year and he was behind a few of today's rivals over C&D last week.
runners.comment: Prolific winner but not found his best so far in 2026; others appeal more for win purposes

runners.name: Impressor
runners.spotlight: Solid effort back from a break when fourth of 17 at Doncaster last month (5f, good to soft); not well drawn at Musselburgh on Sunday and ran okay in the circumstances; chance would be enhanced by significant rain.
runners.comment: Solid 4th at Doncaster and then poorly drawn last time; claims enhanced if rain arrives

runners.name: Hurt You Never
runners.spotlight: Prolific winner but her record at this track is 0-7; should be sharper with her recent C&D return behind her but she may need more weight off her back before she's adding to her 14 victories.
runners.comment: Prolific winner but she needs to leave her recent reappearance behind her; 0-7 at Thirsk

races.race_name: Join Racing TV Today Handicap (Div I)

runners.name: Melvin Udall
runners.spotlight: Lightly-raced 4yo; attracted support and made a perfect start for new connections when seeing off 14 rivals at Doncaster three weeks ago (7f, good to soft); 5lb higher in a better race but there was a lot to like about the way he responded to pressure last time; can do better.
runners.comment: Unexposed 4yo; fine start for new yard with big-field win at Doncaster last month; chance
runners.owner: K Mills, J Brown, S Barley

runners.name: Quest For Fun
runners.spotlight: He finished down the field in this race 12 months ago but he has had the benefit of a run this time around and a promising effort it was too, shuffled back at a crucial stage but keeping on close home (7f, good to soft); his record at York suggests one eye may be on next month's Dante meeting but he's more than capable off this mark.
runners.comment: Shaped nicely on last month's reappearance; on a dangerous mark if that run brings him on

runners.name: One Night Thunder
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer who enjoyed a purple patch last September, winning back-to-back Carlisle handicaps and coming up just shy in his hat-trick bid at Beverley; returns from 170 days off on a tough enough mark and others make more appeal.
runners.comment: In good form last September; returns from 170 days off with a tough enough mark to defy

runners.name: Woodstock
runners.spotlight: Failed to win in 2025 but he ran some solid races in defeat and he makes his seasonal return off a dangerous mark; interesting to see how he fares in the betting.
runners.comment: Winless in 2025 but retained plenty of ability; market useful after 170 days off

runners.name: U Sure Do
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 in a rich vein of form, winning a Catterick handicap (7f, good) before a narrow defeat at Musselburgh (7f, soft) when last seen 164 days ago; another 2lb higher on his reappearance so he will need to be cherry ripe for this return to action.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 on the up; returns on a career-high mark so will need to be cherry ripe

runners.name: Alpha Capture
runners.spotlight: Smart 2yo for Wiliam Haggas; won a Killarney handicap in July 2024 when trained by Willie Mullins; two good runs for new yard this spring, third at Newcastle and Pontefract (both 1m), and likely to be in the shake up.
runners.comment: Two good runs for new yard this spring (1m); drop to 7f should be fine; contender

runners.name: Count Palatine
runners.spotlight: C&D winner; two wins in the second half of 2025 and he ended that campaign with a good second of 15 at Leicester in October; runs off the same mark but he could only manage a midfield finish in this race on his reappearance 12 months ago.
runners.comment: C&D winner; ended 2025 with a good 2nd of 15 at Leicester; likely best watched on return

runners.name: City Captain
runners.spotlight: The winner of four of his eight starts last year (6f, good or slower); perhaps needed the run on his return to action at Newcastle last month, running respectably in the smaller stands' side group; the more rain to fall the better for him.
runners.comment: Four 6f wins last year; should build on last month's reappearance; rain would be a boost

runners.name: Draupnir
runners.spotlight: Lost his action and weakened at Catterick when last seen 199 days ago; prior to that he had comfortably seen off nine rivals in a C&D handicap (soft) in September; only had seven starts and there should be more to come.
runners.comment: C&D winner last September; only had seven runs so could still rate higher

runners.name: Murashah
runners.spotlight: Only raced four times for Charles Hills but he won a 7f novice on his debut and finished second in both handicaps in 2024; the form of the Newbury second worked out extremely well and this mark ought to be in range; a 560-day absence, during which time he has changed hands (30,000gns last July) and been gelded, is the obvious concern but he returns for a stable in red-hot form; hooded first time; betting informative.
runners.comment: Absent 560 days but his new yard are flying along and he looked promising in 2024

runners.name: Frankies Dream
runners.spotlight: Six wins in the first half of 2025, notably when seeing off 21 rivals at York last May; absent for nine months but Paul Mulrennan has been booked for his return (two wins and two seconds from five rides on him) and this should be run to suit.
runners.comment: Six wins last season; returns from nine months off but still relatively unexposed at 7f

runners.name: Beaujolais Nouveau
runners.spotlight: Ran well in the face of a stiff task in a Carlisle Listed event last summer; confirmed she hadn't been flattered there when finishing a fine third over C&D last September; two lesser efforts to round off 2025 but the form of that C&D run, where she travelled so powerfully, offers serious hope this mark is in range; has run well after a break in the past and her trainer is having a fine year; contender.
runners.comment: Has gone well fresh & her C&D 3rd last September suggests she is still well handicapped

runners.name: Leadman
runners.spotlight: Regularly flattered to deceive for Andrew Balding; managed to put his head in front in a 7f Newmarket handicap last July (7f, good to firm) and today's 2lb higher mark not beyond him; he is the type that needs everything to fall right though and others appeal more for win purposes, for all this drop into Class 4 company will be of benefit.
runners.comment: Talented but has a few ideas of his own; can go well fresh; each-way claims down in class

races.race_name: Join Racing TV Today Handicap (Div II)

runners.name: Mudamer
runners.spotlight: Won a pair of 7f handicaps at Catterick last October; returned from a 142-day absence with a fine third at Newcastle three weeks ago, doing best of the smaller stands' side group; runs off the same mark today and should make another bold bid.
runners.comment: Good 3rd on seasonal debut last month (best of stands' side group); same mark here; chance

runners.name: It'sneverjustone
runners.spotlight: Back-to-back wins at Ffos Las and Galway in June/July proved the highlight of his 2025 campaign; promising seasonal return when fourth of 16 at Doncaster three weeks ago; needs to take a step forward.
runners.comment: Promising 4th at Doncaster on his return but he needs to take a step forward to win here
runners.owner: Ian O'Connor & The Fox And Hounds

runners.name: Narmar
runners.spotlight: Did well once cheekpieces were added last summer, winning at Ffos Las (1m, good to soft) and in the frame on four other occasions; can get it wrong at the start and that may be an issue dropping back to 7f.
runners.comment: Did well last summer when cheekpieces were added; drop in trip not sure to suit

runners.name: Packetofbiscuits
runners.spotlight: Arrives in good form, winning twice at Southwell in February and finding only another thriving rival too strong in his hat-trick bid last month; only fifth of 16 back on turf at Doncaster three weeks ago (behind It'sneverjustone); perhaps too high in the weights for now.
runners.comment: Had a good spell on AW this year but latest Doncaster fifth needs bettering
runners.owner: A Pot To Pete In Syndicate

runners.name: Green Pursuit
runners.spotlight: Began 2025 in good form, winning a handicap at Catterick and then finishing a good second at Ripon (both 6f, good); modest form in two runs back with Seb Spencer but his return to action at Doncaster three weeks ago certainly didn't get to the bottom of him, travelling well under restraint and then meeting some trouble as the race was unfolding; capable of better, perhaps markedly so, and the betting should be revealing.
runners.comment: Well held on seasonal return but that hardly tells the tale; betting instructive

runners.name: Stanage
runners.spotlight: Now 9lb lower than for his Doncaster win last March (7f, good); never dangerous in the same race three weeks ago but that run should have sharpened him up; better expected today.
runners.comment: Never dangerous at Doncaster on his reappearance; good mark if that run has brought him on

runners.name: Warm Spell
runners.spotlight: Ran well on his seasonal/stable debut last March; placed in 7f handicaps at Sandown and Kempton on final two runs in 2025; returns in a competitive event.
runners.comment: Placed in 3 of his 5 runs in 2025, including seasonal debut; returns in a competitive race

runners.name: Geo
runners.spotlight: Not on a bad mark but her low-key stable debut at Musselburgh on Sunday doesn't suggest she is the answer this time.
runners.comment: Not on a bad mark but Sunday's stable debut was a low-key one

runners.name: Society Man
runners.spotlight: None too consistent for current stable and his best efforts in Britain have been on AW; ideally suited by testing ground when trained in France so rain would aid his cause.
runners.comment: Best form for current stable has come on AW; best turf form on slower going

runners.name: Kosometsuke
runners.spotlight: Won two handicaps for Roger Varian last year (7f, AW and 6f, soft); only fourth off this mark when last seen in October; sold for 34,000gns later that month; market to guide after his break.
runners.comment: Two handicap wins for R Varian last year; sold 34,000gns since latest; betting to guide

runners.name: War Howl
runners.spotlight: Two 7f wins for Mick Appleby last summer; back to his last winning mark but that is a result of showing little in three starts for current connections; only of interest if attracting support.
runners.comment: No impact in 3 runs for new connections; on a winning mark but only of interest if backed
runners.owner: Future Champions Racing Syn & Ben Haslam

runners.name: Son
runners.spotlight: The winner of just one of his 16 starts for Richard Hannon but he was often highly tried; unplaced in six runs in 2025 but that resulted in his handicap mark dropping 17lb; sold for 24,000gns in October; one of two making their debut for Tim Easterby.
runners.comment: Useful at two and three for R Hannon but 2025 was a struggle; sold 20,000gns since latest

runners.name: Brighton Boy
runners.spotlight: Promising 2yo; needed a drop into selling company, albeit a hot seller, to get off the mark in 2025 (7.6f, soft); sold out of Andrew Balding's stable for 20,000gns last October; could probably do with a few pounds off his back; has worn cheekpieces in the past but goes without today.
runners.comment: Doesn't look to have much in hand of the assessor now starting out for Tim Easterby

races.race_name: Happy 21st Birthday Lily Watkinson Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Relocal
runners.spotlight: Looked on the up when gaining a breakthrough victory at Chelmsford (1m5f) in January; hasn't matched that form both runs since but he failed to last home when fifth in 2m Lingfield handicap last time; not out of things in a change of headgear over this shorter trip.
runners.comment: Failed to stay when fifth over 2m at Lingfield latest; not out of things over shorter trip

runners.name: Hackney Diamonds
runners.spotlight: Built on earlier promise (in a change of headgear) when cosily landing 1m3f Southwell handicap 16 days ago; up 2lb but not taken lightly.
runners.comment: Cosily landed 1m3f Southwell handicap 16 days ago; up 2lb but not taken lightly

runners.name: Lightning Tiger
runners.spotlight: Made a winning start in handicaps at Catterick (1m4f) in October and not discredited off a 6lb higher mark given he went keenly when fourth at Nottingham (1m6f) in final run; this son of Waldgeist could have more to offer in 2026.
runners.comment: Solid fourth at Nottingham final run; still low mileage and could have more to offer

runners.name: Nightsinwhitesatin
runners.spotlight: A dual 1m2f scorer in 2025 and she signed off with an excellent second in 1m4f Catterick handicap in October; yard continues to go very well so she's a likely player returning off the same mark here.
runners.comment: Very good second at Catterick in October; yard going very well so she's a likely player
runners.owner: David Kilburn, Mrs Corbett, Chris Wright

runners.name: Made All
runners.spotlight: C&D scorer who got back to winning ways at Wolverhampton (1m4f) in March; beat just two in 1m4f Musselburgh handicap last time though so needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: C&D scorer; beat just two in Musselburgh handicap last time though so needs to bounce back

runners.name: Don Simon
runners.spotlight: Remains winless but he comes here in good nick, second of six in 2m Southwell handicap last month; effective at this shorter trip and needs considering eased 1lb.
runners.comment: Remains winless but he comes here in good nick; needs considering eased 1lb

runners.name: Idyllic
runners.spotlight: A two-time scorer, including over C&D in the first half of 2025 but she ended last year under something of a cloud; needs to hit the ground running on her seasonal return.
runners.comment: C&D winner but she ended 2025 under something of a cloud; needs to hit the ground running

runners.name: Minhad
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark at Newmarket (1m4f) last April and in good form in handicaps after until coming in last of 12 back at Newmarket (1m4f) in October; since gelded and goes without usual headgear on his return.
runners.comment: In good form until last at Newmarket in October; since gelded and without usual headgear

runners.name: Helmsley
runners.spotlight: Ended her time with Ralph Beckett with 1m2f Redcar novice success but she has failed to fire subsequently for Ivan Furtado, only sixth of eight in 1m Newcastle handicap last time; tried back over a much longer trip now.
runners.comment: Has failed to fire for Ivan Furtado, only sixth at Newcastle latest; back over longer trip

runners.name: Cougar
runners.spotlight: Revitalised in blinkers and ended 2025 with 1m4f handicap victories at Musselburgh and Hamilton in September; up 5lb for his reappearance but yard is in good form and can make his presence felt.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 with 1m4f wins at Musselburgh and Hamilton; up 5lb for can make presence felt

runners.name: Furhaan
runners.spotlight: Made a winning start in handicaps at Windsor (11.5f) in September and backed it up with a solid fifth at Yarmouth (11.5f) the following month on his final run for Ed Walker; since changed hands for 32,000gns and he can't be discounted starting out for another good yard here with few miles still on the clock.
runners.comment: Solid Yarmouth fifth final run for Ed Walker; not discounted on stable debut

runners.name: Gallivanted
runners.spotlight: A fair 1m4f scorer for Tom Clover who has been in good (winning) form sent hurdling for his current stable this winter, third at Ascot 20 days ago; not out of things on his handicap debut back in this sphere.
runners.comment: In winning form over hurdles for current stable so not out of things on his handicap debut

races.race_name: Stirrup Bar For Deli-Style Dining Packages Handicap

runners.name: Simplify
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with an easy 6f novice win here on good ground; game effort to win a 5f handicap at Wolverhampton on her return 18 days ago but she would be no banker to confirm the placings with The Lost Sock on 3lb worse terms.
runners.comment: 6f win here in September; followed up at Wolverhampton (5f) last month; this is tougher

runners.name: Merlier
runners.spotlight: He has form figures of 211 but in fields of four, five and three; his ceiling is not easy to establish but there is no doubt he is in much deeper on this turf and handicap debut.
runners.comment: Promising in small-field AW events; unexposed but this race is considerably tougher

runners.name: The Lost Sock
runners.spotlight: 5f win at Catterick on final 2yo start; flopped when strong favourite at Kempton on seasonal/handicap debut last month (too free over 6f); better effort when third at Wolverhampton 18 days ago (5f), having to wait for a gap to appear but keeping on when in the clear; still has some potential back on turf.
runners.comment: Beaten fav in 2 AW runs last month but latest 3rd was a decent effort; chance back on turf

runners.name: Exclamation
runners.spotlight: Doncaster novice winner (5f, good to soft) last July and ran well for second in a soft-ground nursery at Sandown one month later; highly tried since and he has come up short, albeit running a good race when fifth in the Group 2 Flying Childers; drops back into a handicap but he has to concede weight to some promising sprinters.
runners.comment: Return to handicap company in his favour but he looks to face a tough task off top weight

runners.name: Rikki Tiki Tavi
runners.spotlight: Made all when beating The Lost Sock by a head over C&D last August; came up short in stronger races twice afterwards but there were mitigating factors; one of three runners for Kevin Ryan.
runners.comment: C&D win last August; excuses in stronger races twice after; not fully exposed; yard runs 3

runners.name: Big Fun
runners.spotlight: Didn't have to improve to win a four-runner Musselburgh maiden last July (5f, good; at 1-6); creditable seventh in the Super Sprint at Newbury 18 days later; absent since; one of three runners for Kevin Ryan.
runners.comment: Promising at two, running well in the Super Sprint when last seen; stable runs three here

runners.name: Mo Of Cairo
runners.spotlight: Won a Beverley maiden (5f, good to firm) in July and went on from there to run a stormer in a 6f nursery at York's Ebor meeting, showing bright speed and headed only late on; that form is rock solid and he looks a key player despite a 3lb rise.
runners.comment: Last seen finishing a close 2nd at York's Ebor meeting (6f); up 3lb but far from exposed

runners.name: Hanney Girl
runners.spotlight: Two 5f wins on fast turf last summer; finished second at Chester in her hat-trick bid in September and she is only 1lb higher today; stable also runs Go Vince Go who beat this filly at York when she was last seen.
runners.comment: Speedy filly who did well as a 2yo; needs to up her game to take this after a layoff

runners.name: Schrodinger's Cat
runners.spotlight: Seventh time lucky for Harry Charlton when narrowly landing a 5f novice at Redcar 214 days ago; sold for 30,000gns in October; makes his handicap, seasonal and stable debut in a competitive event.
runners.comment: Consistent rather than progressive for H Charlton; sold 30,000gns since latest; opposable

runners.name: Entailed
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner but unplaced in two nurseries for Andrew Balding; sold for 27,000gns in October; looks up against it on her stable debut.
runners.comment: Maiden winner for Andrew Balding; sold 27,000gns in October; looks on a tough mark

runners.name: Go Vince Go
runners.spotlight: Raced over 5f only once as a 2yo but it produced his best performance, coming from rear to win a York nursery in October; 5lb higher today but this is set to be strongly run and coming from off the pace might be beneficial; interesting.
runners.comment: Peak 2yo effort came on his only attempt at 5f; this should be run to suit; interesting

runners.name: Daneh Of Dandy
runners.spotlight: Two wins as a 2yo, including C&D; slightly disappointing on her seasonal and handicap debut at Wolverhampton last month but it's possible the return to turf will see her in a better light.
runners.comment: Two turf wins last year, including C&D; disappointed on seasonal/handicap/AW debut

runners.name: Dazzling Haze
runners.spotlight: Won two of her four nursery runs for the Crisfords, including over C&D in September (good ground); improvement necessary to overcome this mark but she can't be ruled out on her first run for Robert Cowell.
runners.comment: Two nursery wins for the Crisfords, including C&D; not ruled out starting out for new yard

runners.name: Star Material
runners.spotlight: Of interest on his two best efforts last year, namely a 5f novice win and a good second in a nursery at the Ayr Gold Cup meeting; market useful back from a six-month break.
runners.comment: Of interest on last season's two best efforts; returns from a break in a deep race

races.race_name: Hunt Cup Next Meeting Saturday 2nd May Handicap

runners.name: Doon The Glen
runners.spotlight: Finally scored at Newcastle (6f) last March; off afterwards until the turn of the year but he got back on track when second in 5f classified event returned to Newcastle last month; possibilities.
runners.comment: Back on track when second in 5f classified event at Newcastle latest; possibilities

runners.name: Wee Mary
runners.spotlight: Gained her breakthrough win in 5f classified event at Newcastle in January but she's failed to build on it, only seventh in similar event there last month; usual hood is retried now (last on for her win).
runners.comment: Breakthrough win in Newcastle classified event in January but has failed to build on it

runners.name: La Rochette
runners.spotlight: On a long losing sequence and she was retried in cheekpieces when beating just one in 5f Newcastle handicap in February last time; others appeal more.
runners.comment: On a long losing sequence and beat one in 5f Newcastle handicap in February last time

runners.name: Mister McGregor
runners.spotlight: 0-22 and he beat just two in 6f classified event at Wolverhampton last time; blinkers are now tried on this unreliable sort.
runners.comment: 0-22 and he beat two in 6f classified event at Wolverhampton last time; blinkers go on

runners.name: Piperstown
runners.spotlight: In fine form on the AW this winter, landing back-to-back 6f handicaps at Wolverhampton and Southwell recently; very lightly raced on turf but still merits serious consideration in his hat-trick bid.
runners.comment: In fine form on the AW this winter; very lightly raced on turf but player in hat-trick bid

runners.name: Tommytwohoots
runners.spotlight: On a long losing sequence but he has started life well for John Mackie on last two outings, fourth of 12 in 5f Catterick handicap ten days ago; very much one to consider off the same mark with cheekpieces refitted.
runners.comment: On a long losing run but has started life well for John Mackie; very much one to consider

runners.name: Eldeyaar
runners.spotlight: On a lengthy losing sequence and he came in only 11th in 6f Wolverhampton handicap 19 days ago; tried in different headgear here.
runners.comment: On a lengthy losing sequence and he came in 11th in 6f Wolverhampton handicap 19 days ago

runners.name: Run This Way
runners.spotlight: It's now 13 outings since her last win and she ended 2025 with a pair of below-par efforts in 6f Newcastle handicaps; this C&D winner needs to take a step forward despite a falling mark.
runners.comment: It's now 13 outings since her last win; this C&D winner needs to take a step forward

runners.name: Stormy Pearl
runners.spotlight: Scored at Hamilton (5f) last July but she ended 2025 with four below-par efforts; others are preferred on this occasion.
runners.comment: Scored at Hamilton last July but she ended 2025 with four below-par efforts

runners.name: What What What
runners.spotlight: Had the headgear left off when back to winning ways in 6f Southwell handicap 53 days ago; he's yet to show much in a handful of turf runs though, so needs to back it up.
runners.comment: Won at Southwell latest; yet to show much in a handful of turf runs though

runners.name: Golden Prosperity
runners.spotlight: Back on song to land 5f Catterick handicap ten days ago; this C&D scorer needs to back it up off a 3lb higher mark.
runners.comment: Back on song to land 5f Catterick h'cap ten days ago; this C&D scorer needs to back it up

runners.name: Langholm
runners.spotlight: Course winner but he ended last season with a poor effort in 7f Newcastle handicap; this veteran has gone well fresh though, so is no forlorn hope on his reappearance.
runners.comment: Course winner; has gone well fresh though, so is no forlorn hope on his reappearance

runners.name: Hurstwood
runners.spotlight: A dual 6f scorer in 2025 but he also didn't prove the easiest to catch right; needs to hit the ground running after 165 days off.
runners.comment: A dual 6f scorer in 2025 but didn't prove easy to catch right; needs to hit ground running
runners.owner: Hard Held Partnership And Angus Racing

runners.name: Siviez
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage filly who was campaigned at 7f/1m until a running-on sixth of 12 in 5f Catterick handicap ten days ago; interesting for her in-form stable now back up to 6f.
runners.comment: Running-on sixth in 5f Catterick h'cap latest; interesting for in-form yard now up to 6f

runners.name: Canaria Sun
runners.spotlight: Poor maiden who beat one in 5f Pontefract handicap when last seen out in October; needs to hit the ground running after six months off.
runners.comment: Beat one in 5f Pontefract h'cap when last seen in October; needs to hit the ground running

runners.name: Emerald Army
runners.spotlight: Took this on his return 12 months ago for Declan Carroll but he ended last term largely below par; starts out for Ruth Carr here and no surprise to see him bounce back.
runners.comment: Took this on return year ago for Declan Carroll; debut for Ruth Carr and could bounce back

runners.name: Jet Warrior
runners.spotlight: Cut little ice in a pair of 1m2f maidens in Ireland for Natalia Lupini and last of five for his new yard in 7f Newcastle novice in February; yard is going well but this switch to handicaps needs to spark major progress.
runners.comment: Last of five for new yard at Newcastle, so switch to h'caps needs to spark major progress

races.race_name: Broxton Gates Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Knock Off Soxs
runners.spotlight: Advanced his form on his handicap debut when third of ten at Exeter (good to soft) but wasn't so good at Taunton (good); has a chance on the figures back at novice level if he's back on song.
runners.comment: Has a chance on the figures back at novice level if he's back on song after last time

runners.name: Alsakib
runners.spotlight: Very smart Flat horse for Andrew Balding; had wind surgery after his one blip over hurdles and then won at Musselburgh despite a slipping saddle (2m1f, good to soft); carries a penalty but looks a major player.
runners.comment: Very smart Flat horse; won at Musselburgh despite a slipping saddle

runners.name: Senator
runners.spotlight: Placed in two French Flat races; sent off evens when narrowly accounting for a useful rival in a Market Rasen maiden two months ago (soft) and was withdrawn with a self certificate from a traditionally strong 4yo handicap at Ascot last month; can go well.
runners.comment: Market Rasen winner who was a late withdrawal from an Ascot handicap last month

runners.name: Static Charm
runners.spotlight: Shown enough in bumpers to believe he can win races but perhaps not this one.
runners.comment: Shown enough in bumpers to believe he can win races but perhaps not this one

runners.name: Tunisya
runners.spotlight: Won a French hurdle on heavy ground; only 5-2 for British/stable debut but faded on the soft ground at Wetherby; these conditions may be more to her liking, for all that she has won in the mud.
runners.comment: French winner on heavy; faded on soft at Wetherby; drier ground may help

runners.name: Laristote
runners.spotlight: Respectable run at Sedgefield on the back of a wind operation but the RPR was nothing to shout about and he'll need more here.
runners.comment: Latest RPR was nothing to shout about and he'll need more here

runners.name: Phantom Gold
runners.spotlight: Won two bumpers, one in the pointing scene and the other under rules; however, finished tailed off in a 2m4f maiden hurdle at Sedgefield on the back of a mammoth absence.
runners.comment: Bumper winner but tailed off on hurdle debut after a mammoth absence

runners.name: Chico Magnifica
runners.spotlight: 2-4 on the Flat for Karl Burke and quite useful; no sign of that ability over hurdles and probably one for handicaps.
runners.comment: No sign of good Flat ability over hurdles and probably one for handicaps

runners.name: Saxon Cross
runners.spotlight: Showed a nice of ability in bumpers for former Irish yard but was weak in the betting and tailed off on hurdle/stable debut at Huntingdon.
runners.comment: Weak in the betting and tailed off on hurdle/stable debut at Huntingdon

runners.name: L'Hybris Is Born
runners.spotlight: Very opposable on his pointing exploits.
runners.comment: Very opposable on his pointing exploits

runners.name: Anxious Moments
runners.spotlight: Running well when unseating four out in an Irish point last month and clearly has a level of ability; market useful.
runners.comment: Running well when unseating four out in an Irish point last month

runners.name: Cappa Lass
runners.spotlight: Placed in her second point; hooded for hurdle debut at Haydock (good; 14-1) and was tailed off under Harry Cobden.
runners.comment: Hooded for hurdle debut at Haydock (good) and was tailed off under Harry Cobden

runners.name: Karthala
runners.spotlight: Modest Flat maiden who offered little on hurdle debut at Huntingdon two weeks ago.
runners.comment: Modest Flat maiden who offered little on hurdle debut at Huntingdon two weeks ago

races.race_name: Gieves & Hawkes Novices' Handicap Chase

runners.name: El Rojo Grande
runners.spotlight: Has more chasing experience than many of these and with a strike-rate of 4-15 things are going well; those wins have been from 2m to 2m4f so the are stamina questions to answer, for all that he has won a 3m point.
runners.comment: Experienced chaser and 4-15; a winner last time out over 2m4f; some stamina worries
runners.owner: Genghis Racing & Mike Harris Racing Club

runners.name: Kapamazov
runners.spotlight: 21 defeats under rules include 13 over fences but he's been running well, latterly finishing third on yard debut at Hereford (2m5f, good); stays this far.
runners.comment: 21 defeats under rules include 13 over fences but he's been running better than most

runners.name: Subtle Fortune
runners.spotlight: Rules wins (including one chase) came on soft or heavy but it was good ground when close up behind Streamsforth Lad over C&D last month; good chance.
runners.comment: It was good ground when close up behind Streamsforth Lad over C&D last month

runners.name: Tory Hill
runners.spotlight: In new cheekpieces when second to a thriving rival at Carlisle (3m, good) and won't be far away if as good again.
runners.comment: In new cheekpieces when second to a thriving rival at Carlisle (3m, good)

runners.name: Black Occ
runners.spotlight: Placed effort over hurdles (3m1f) divides his two chase runs and was a 3l runner-up at Hereford last time (2m5f, good); more appealing than a good many.
runners.comment: More appealing than a good many after finishing runner-up at Hereford last time
runners.owner: P Conway, T Lewis & G Wilson

runners.name: Atlanta Brave
runners.spotlight: Has become bitterly disappointing but handicapped accordingly and his third at Hereford was better (3m1f, good); cheekpieces are back on but they'd done little for him previously.
runners.comment: Has become disappointing but his third at Hereford was better (3m1f, good)

runners.name: Streamsforth Lad
runners.spotlight: Jumping has been an issue but it's improved in his last two races when runner-up behind winners who were well backed and well handicapped; has course form; big shout.
runners.comment: Runner-up to well backed and well handicapped winners in last two races; big shout

runners.name: Rockvale
runners.spotlight: Jumped moderately when beaten 25l on chase debut two runs back and again well held over hurdles here when tried in a hood.
runners.comment: Jumped moderately when beaten 25l on chase debut two runs back; poor since over hurdles

runners.name: Sandscape
runners.spotlight: This is a workable mark on the best of his hurdle form (maiden) and, although disappointing on his chase debut, he did go off favourite; respected in the new cheekpieces.
runners.comment: On a workable mark on the best of his hurdle form; quiet on chase debut; in cheekpieces

runners.name: Shuile Warrior
runners.spotlight: Only 1-8 in points; no worthwhile form under rules and now makes chase debut.
runners.comment: Only 1-8 in points; no worthwhile form under rules and now makes chase debut

runners.name: Harksman
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden who showed a bit more last time when beaten 8l in a 2m6f handicap hurdle at Stratford; that was his first run in cheekpieces as well; chase debut.
runners.comment: Showed a bit more last time when beaten 8l in a 2m6f handicap hurdle at Stratford

runners.name: Huckleberry Sting
runners.spotlight: Poor five-race maiden over hurdles; has won a point but unconvincing claims on chase debut.
runners.comment: Poor five-race maiden over hurdles; has won a point but unconvincing claims on chase debut

runners.name: Siege Of Acres
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced hurdler; ran well in his first chase and was doing likewise when falling at Sedgefield, though wouldn't have won; others preferred.
runners.comment: Running well when falling at Sedgefield, though he wouldn't have won

runners.name: Peckforton Hills
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner; brings momentum into chase debut after three consecutive placed efforts over hurdles, in this headgear combination in his final two races (2m3f/3m); clearly a contender if taking to fences.
runners.comment: Placed in last three hurdle races and clearly a contender if taking to fences

races.race_name: Bangor-On-Dee Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Reckless Spending
runners.spotlight: Ran quite well at Ascot in November but the wide-margin Kempton win in February (2m5f, good to soft) is his standout piece of form; ran poorly in a Grade 2 over 3m last time but that couldn't have been his true running.
runners.comment: Ran poorly in a Grade 2 over 3m last time but that couldn't have been his true running
runners.owner: Mrs G Van Geest & M George

runners.name: Park Princess
runners.spotlight: When a wide-margin Listed race winner at Doncaster it was against her own sex and very few in that eight-runner race appeared to handle the deteriorating conditions; went off 50-1 for the Albert Bartlett and performed accordingly; 3-6 over hurdles and she's a contender with her sights lowered.
runners.comment: 3-6 over hurdles and contender with sights lowered (pulled up in Albert Bartlett latest)

runners.name: Pottersville
runners.spotlight: Won by a wide margin at Exeter (2m7f, good to soft) in November but has finished last in her subsequent races, latterly in a 3m Grade 2 (50-1); retains potential but has been off for nearly three months.
runners.comment: Impressed for the win but finished last in final two races, the latest a Grade 2

runners.name: Patrick's Wells
runners.spotlight: Point winner but was tailed off on hurdle debut and then a remote third two weeks ago when returning from a long absence.
runners.comment: A remote third two weeks ago when returning from a long absence

runners.name: Johnny Moonshine
runners.spotlight: Looks outclassed by the best of these judged on what he's shown in a bumper and two maidens; the longer trip could help as he was second in an Irish point.
runners.comment: Outclassed by the best of these judged on what he's shown in a bumper and two maidens

races.race_name: Keegans Independent Drinks Suppliers Handicap Hurdle (Div I)

runners.name: Killycarn
runners.spotlight: Has won a handicap, at Catterick on New Year's Day (1m7f, good); back to something like that form when second to an improver at Musselburgh (2m4f, good to soft); can go well.
runners.comment: Back to something like his winning form when second to an improver at Musselburgh

runners.name: Crack Ops
runners.spotlight: Useful bumper form on good ground, winning at Ludlow and sixth of 16 at Cheltenham (Listed); yet to replicate that over hurdles but chased home an unexposed Henderson horse here last time; handicap debut.
runners.comment: Chased home an unexposed Henderson horse here last time; handicap debut

runners.name: I've Madeupmymind
runners.spotlight: Course winner last February; bang there when falling two out on handicap debut at Kempton (3m, good) in November but she's struggled since and been all over the shop trip-wise.
runners.comment: Course winner; struggled in last three races and been all over the shop trip-wise

runners.name: Sutherland
runners.spotlight: Made mistakes when third in a Newbury handicap (2m, good to soft) but his Southwell run over 2m4f (good) was disappointing when tried in cheekpieces (persevered with); already looking hard to predict.
runners.comment: His Newbury third gives him major claims but he's already looking hard to predict

runners.name: They Want Me
runners.spotlight: Hasn't kicked on from the maiden win at Kelso (2m, good to soft) but this is his handicap debut over a longer trip and he did beat the useful Swindon Village in an Irish point; tries a tongue-tie after wind surgery.
runners.comment: Maiden winner; up in trip for handicap debut and tries a tongue-tie after wind surgery

runners.name: Shengai Enki
runners.spotlight: Has the form to feature off this mark but in two runs for this yard he's been tailed off over hurdles and pulled up on chase debut.
runners.comment: In two runs for this yard he's been tailed off over hurdles and pulled up on chase debut

runners.name: Lejo Du Seuil
runners.spotlight: The ground was heavy when showing improved form in a Huntingdon maiden (2m) two months ago but he might be improving anyway; this mark might not be too bad for his handicap debut.
runners.comment: Improved second at Huntingdon and this mark might not be too bad for his handicap debut

runners.name: Locquirec
runners.spotlight: Ex-French 5yo who remains lightly raced; he was on the retreat when falling in his second handicap at Newbury (2m, good to soft; Sutherland was third) but he ran well to a point and probably has a bigger effort in him.
runners.comment: He was on the retreat when falling in his second handicap at Newbury; lightly raced

runners.name: Vinnie O'Neil
runners.spotlight: Hard to make a case after finishing tailed off when 40-1 for his handicap debut (2m3f, soft; first-time tongue-tie); did show a smidgeon of promise on good ground last year but he's unconvincing.
runners.comment: Hard to make a case after finishing tailed off when 40-1 for his handicap debut

races.race_name: Keegans Independent Drinks Suppliers Handicap Hurdle (Div II)

runners.name: Tramuntana
runners.spotlight: Had a wind operation since pulling up on chase debut last time (first-time cheekpieces now dispensed with); bumper/hurdle winner who had been in good form in handicaps prior to that.
runners.comment: In good form in handicaps prior to chase debut latest; since had wind surgery

runners.name: Eremenko
runners.spotlight: It wasn't a strong maiden she won at Plumpton (2m4f, good) but she breezed home despite a late mistake; not the biggest of mares but well bred and should be able to cope with this opening mark.
runners.comment: Recent maiden winner; not the biggest but well bred and obvious claims on handicap debut

runners.name: Good Boy Griff
runners.spotlight: Hasn't looked devoid of ability in bumpers or over hurdles and could take a step forward now into handicaps.
runners.comment: Hasn't looked devoid of ability and could take a step forward now into handicaps

runners.name: Gimarias
runners.spotlight: 66-1 when fifth in a C&D maiden here last month when about 10l behind Good Boy Griff; he wouldn't be the obvious answer.
runners.comment: 66-1 when fifth in a C&D maiden here last month when about 10l behind Good Boy Griff
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Mr A Rutherford

runners.name: Hang On
runners.spotlight: Only had six races and the latest on handicap debut was after an absence of 837 days; shaped well at Carlisle (2m3f, good to soft), finishing 14l off the winner after getting outpaced; should be all the sharper now.
runners.comment: Encouraging handicap debut 14 days ago after an absence; should be sharper now

runners.name: I C U In My Dreams
runners.spotlight: Another handicap debutante who looks progressive having had a Newcastle maiden (2m4f, good to soft) sewn up some way out; this mark isn't a gift but open to further improvement.
runners.comment: Made all in latest maiden; this mark no gift but open to further improvement

runners.name: Titanium Ring
runners.spotlight: Second to recent Aintree winner Forthfactor on bumper debut; no match for the winner on his handicap debut over C&D (good) but it was a fair start to life in races of this nature; considered.
runners.comment: No match for the winner on his handicap debut here but it was an encouraging effort

runners.name: Las Canals
runners.spotlight: French bumper winner; bled from the nose two runs ago and left that behind with a hood added (retained) when springing a 33-1 surprise in a Sedgefield maiden hurdle (2m1f, good) last month; unexposed but doesn't look obviously well treated for this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Unexposed but doesn't look obviously well treated for this handicap debut

runners.name: Diamond Jim
runners.spotlight: Easy winner of a Haydock juvenile hurdle and then fourth in another when penalised; departed early on handicap debut at Sedgefield (2m1f, good; first-time cheekpieces) when strong in the betting at 9-4; has to be of interest.
runners.comment: Juvenile hurdle winner; departed early on handicap debut did go off at only 9-4

races.race_name: Broxton Gates Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Jorebel
runners.spotlight: Dual Fontwell winner and his other victories have come on left-handed tracks; hung in that direction on last month's visits to Kempton and he duly underperformed; should run better around here and this mark isn't beyond him.
runners.comment: This mark isn't beyond him and returning to a left-handed track looks a good thing

runners.name: Carrigeen Castle
runners.spotlight: On a good mark but hard to predict and this looks stronger than the race in which he ran second at Hexham.
runners.comment: This looks stronger than the race in which he ran second at Hexham

runners.name: Doyouknowwhatimean
runners.spotlight: Ex-Dan Skelton; went close on yard debut in December (2m4f, heavy) but then twice pulled up and was beaten 14l here 18 days ago (good); wouldn't be the percentage call.
runners.comment: One of his better runs when beaten 14l here last time but not the percentage call

runners.name: Duel Au Soleil
runners.spotlight: Combination of handicaps, a wind operation and this headgear has made a different, delivering form figures of 132 in 2m5f-3m handicap hurdles before faring less well after 97 days off at Wetherby; hard to be confident on the back of that but no surprise should he run well now his attention is turned to fences.
runners.comment: First three handicap runs all positive and latest run was after a break; chase debut

runners.name: Lady Jago
runners.spotlight: Won three small-field chases at up to 3m1f for Dan Skelton; bought for £10,000 in July and hasn't recaptured her best form for new yard.
runners.comment: Bought for £10,000 in July and hasn't recaptured her best form for new yard

runners.name: Admiral Stewart
runners.spotlight: Some lengthy breaks in his lightly raced profile but it was a positive first run back from wind surgery when beaten 3.5l at Uttoxter three weeks ago (2m4f, good to soft); the burning question is whether he'll be as good again.
runners.comment: Close 4th after a wind op last time; burning question is whether he'll be as good again

runners.name: My Noble Lord
runners.spotlight: Won at Uttoxeter (2m4f, soft) in January when wearing first-time cheekpieces (retained) and subsequent defeats have come in better races than this; didn't run at all badly in the latest at Kelso (2m5f); back down in grade and with blinkers given a go, he's feared.
runners.comment: Winner three starts back; back down in grade and with blinkers given a go, he's feared

runners.name: Presentandcounting
runners.spotlight: Returned to winning ways at Uttoxeter (2m4f, good) last summer and remained competitive afterwards; however, he has since turned 12 and lacking a run might not be ideal.
runners.comment: In good form last year but since turned 12 and lacking a run might not be ideal

races.race_name: Overton Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Carismatic Soldier
runners.spotlight: Won a novice hurdle at Chepstow (2m, heavy) last January but he's not really gone on from that and has been well held in his last three handicaps; still has low mileage but he needs improvement back up in trip; first-time blinkers replace cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 7yo but he's been well held in last three handicaps; blinkers are now tried

runners.name: Dwight K Schrute
runners.spotlight: Record of 1-9 over hurdles but he found more improvement with a near-miss in a Hereford handicap (3m2f, good) last month; nudged up 2lb but he's building up a solid profile in staying handicaps and is a key player.
runners.comment: Record of 1-9 over hurdles but he had a near-miss at Hereford (3m2f) latest; key player

runners.name: Kintail
runners.spotlight: Ex-Nicky Henderson; just 1-10 over hurdles and has struggled with consistency but he ran well to finish third at Hereford (3m2f; first-time blinkers) last month; ties in with Dwight K Schrute on that form and has claims if he can back that up.
runners.comment: Ties in with Dwight K Schrute on Hereford form and has claims if he can back that up

runners.name: Robert d'Ores
runners.spotlight: All three hurdle wins have been on slow ground at Hexham (2m7f) and latest was in October; only 2lb higher than for that success but he's been well held in his last two starts; may have needed the run at Carlisle last month but this is competitive and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Hexham specialist who has been well held in his last two runs and others are preferred

runners.name: Our Dylan
runners.spotlight: Veteran who has won three of his last five starts and was as good as ever when scoring on the step up to 3m at Wincanton (good) last month; up 4lb but he should have more to offer over staying trips and he's an interesting contender.
runners.comment: Has won three of his last five and was as good as ever when scoring over 3m last time

runners.name: Golden Glance
runners.spotlight: Sole win was in a Haydock maiden (2m3f, good to soft) in April 2023 and she's finished tailed off in both runs since returning from a long absence in December; has plenty to prove and she's untried at this trip.
runners.comment: Back from long absence with two tailed-off efforts and she's untried at this trip

runners.name: Speed Davis
runners.spotlight: Has a patchy record but he made it 2-10 over hurdles when justifying support at Musselburgh (2m4f, good to soft) in November; 6lb higher on his return but that was a comfortable success and he looks worth a try at this new trip; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Ended last year with a win at Musselburgh (2m4f); respected upped in trip on his return

runners.name: Tiger Orchid
runners.spotlight: Multiple winner for the Newland/Insole yard; good second over fences on stable debut at Hereford (3m1f, soft) in February and he backed that up with a close call over hurdles at Wetherby (3m, good to soft) last month; 2lb higher here but he remains feasibly treated and won't mind what the weather does; respected.
runners.comment: Second in both runs for current yard including a close call over hurdles latest; respected

runners.name: Hillview
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who is well treated on his old form but he had a long spell on the sidelines before struggling at big prices in both runs this winter; hard to know how much ability remains and he has plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Had long spell on the sidelines before struggling in both runs this winter; lots to prove

races.race_name: Family Funday On 16th May "Junior" National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Enya
runners.spotlight: Runner-up over hurdles at Warwick (2m, good to soft) in November and she's returned after wind surgery with two frame efforts in bumpers this spring; was only beaten around 5l when fourth at Newton Abbot (2m1f, good) last time and she has improved with each of her three runs; possibilities.
runners.comment: Has shown clear promise in all three runs including two bumpers this spring; possibilities
runners.owner: Sheep As A Lamb Syndicate Gary Gillies

runners.name: Longlands
runners.spotlight: 36,000euros foal, 110,000euros 3yo; fourth foal; brother to point/2m4f hurdle winner Freshers Week; dam twice-raced sister to smart 2m2f-3m chase winner Gold Present, out of unraced half-sister to high-class 2m1f-2m5f hurdle/chase winner Rajdhani Express; yard 25% in bumpers this season and he's an interesting newcomer.
runners.comment: 110,000euros 3yo; yard 25% in bumpers this season and he's an interesting newcomer
runners.owner: Mr And Mrs J D Cotton

runners.name: Tide Is Turning
runners.spotlight: Frontiersman gelding who made a promising start when a clear third at Kempton (2m, good; 17-2) in February; that was behind a useful prospect who was well backed and he sets the standard on that form; respected.
runners.comment: Promising third on Kempton debut in February and he sets the standard on that form

runners.name: Berkshire Woody
runners.spotlight: Made a good start at a biggish price when third at Aintree (2m1f, good to soft) in November; had tough assignment in Listed bumper at Cheltenham in January but he was well below form again at Hereford (2m, soft) the following month; still early days but he has some questions to answer.
runners.comment: Promising third on Aintree debut but he's not gone on from that; bit to prove
runners.owner: Berkshire Parts & Panels Ltd No1 Fanclub

runners.name: Jetzet
runners.spotlight: 115,000euros 3yo; held in third of six when odds-on at Doncaster (2m, good to soft) last month but he travelled smoothly for a long way before his effort flattened out; that was an encouraging start and he's a player if he can build on that.
runners.comment: Turned over at odds-on on Doncaster debut but he travelled well for a long way; in the mix
runners.owner: Julie And David R Martin And Dan Hall

runners.name: Affinitive
runners.spotlight: Out of a half-sister to three useful winners notably Stellar Notion (bumper and 2m-2m7f jumps); well-held fifth at Warwick (good; 13-2) last month but he ran green when it came to the crunch and should know much more this time; looks a likely improver on his second start and he's not ruled out.
runners.comment: Ran better than the bare facts on Warwick debut and he should know more this time

runners.name: Crystal Anthem
runners.spotlight: 38,000euros 3yo; fourth foal; half-brother to bumper/2m3f hurdle winner Just Golden; dam unraced half-sister to bumper and smart jumps winner O O Seven; stable 0-20 in bumpers in last five seasons and he could be one for later on.
runners.comment: 38,000euros 3yo; yard 0-20 in bumpers in recent years and he could be one for later on

runners.name: Emral Bridge
runners.spotlight: Seventh foal; dam bumper and 2m4f-2m5f hurdle/chase winner, half-sister to winners Rattlin (bumper and 2m4f-3m1f hurdle/chase) and Spud (bumper/2m hurdle); yard 21% in bumpers in recent years and he needs watching in market on debut.
runners.comment: Yard 21% in bumpers in recent years and he needs watching in market on debut

runners.name: Mighty Rhythm
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; brother to useful 2m hurdle winner Llandinabo Lad, half-brother to winners King Of Tara (2m hurdle/chase) and Legendary Rhythm (2m hurdle/2m4f chase); dam placed in bumper, half-sister to useful 1m2f/1m4f winner Pentatonic; yard has had a debut bumper winner this season and he needs checking in market.
runners.comment: Brother to a useful hurdle winner and he needs checking in market on debut

runners.name: Upweitjack
runners.spotlight: 9,000euros foal, 36,000euros 3yo; tenth foal; half-brother to winners Last Garrison (1m2f-1m4f; RPR 68), Hyden (1m4f; 66), The Nutcracker (1m2f Flat; useful 2m hurdle) and Big Dispute (bumper); dam 5f 2yo winner (67), half-sister to 5f-6f winner Rigid Rock; has fair standard to aim at on debut and market should guide.
runners.comment: 36,000euros 3yo; has fair standard to aim at on debut and market should guide

runners.name: Littlebobbydazzler
runners.spotlight: Market springer into 9-2 at Hereford (2m, soft) in February and he came from off the pace to finish a 2l third of ten; that was a promising debut and he should benefit from that experience; in the mix.
runners.comment: Promising third on Hereford debut and he should benefit from that experience

runners.name: Pluto Star
runners.spotlight: 80-1 and he finished a remote eighth on his debut at Southwell (2m, soft) in February.
runners.comment: 80-1 and he finished a remote eighth on his debut at Southwell (2m, soft) in February

races.race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Eskimo Pie
runners.spotlight: Winless in sprints as 2yo but had some tough tasks and was beaten only 3l in Group 2 Queen Mary at Royal Ascot; off after July and had stamina as well as fitness to prove on recent return on Kempton AW (1m) where she hung on well for third against strong-finishing rivals; thereabouts.
runners.comment: Winless but had some tough tasks in sprints as 2yo; good return over 1m on AW; thereabouts

runners.name: Deedaydiva
runners.spotlight: Promising 7f debut and she more than backed that up when sticking her neck out for a Sandown win (7f, soft) in August, form that turned out to be useful; can improve for 1m; holds a 1,000 Guineas entry; good claims.
runners.comment: Useful form when winning over 7f at Sandown (soft) in August; has 1,000 Guineas entry

runners.name: Shadida
runners.spotlight: By Palace Pier; fifth foal; half-sister to winners Kaasirr (7f/1m including KSA; RPR 80) and Tarraff (1m4f; 80); dam 6f-1m 2yo winner (including Listed; 103); from a leading yard and the betting should be helpful.
runners.comment: Out of a Listed winner at 1m; from a top yard and the market can guide.

runners.name: Brighlee
runners.spotlight: Showed plenty in two of her first three starts, most significantly over 1m at Salisbury (good to firm) in August; disappointing favourite on AW in October but she should be in contention on the previous turf run.
runners.comment: Showed plenty in 2 of first 3 runs, but disappointing on AW when last seen in October

runners.name: Botagoz
runners.spotlight: 260,000euros yearling out of multiple winner, including in Group 3 and 2s at 1m1f; easy to back for Kempton AW debut (7f) in December but she picked up nicely to take the lead; the bare form wasn't anything dramatic but the extra furlong should suit; in the mix.
runners.comment: 260,000euros yearling; picked up nicely for 7f AW win on debut; extra 1f can suit

runners.name: Midnight Steppers
runners.spotlight: 75,000gns yearling; seventh foal; by Blue Point; winning siblings include Italian 7.5f/1m winner Love Words (including 2yo/Listed; RPR 108) and Irish Lullaby (10.6f-12.3f Flat, 98; hurdle/chase); dam 1m-1m1f winner (including Italian Group 3; 105); no surprise if this is needed.
runners.comment: Italian Listed/Group winners close up in pedigree but likely to benefit from this debut

runners.name: Palace Artois
runners.spotlight: Modest efforts in first two starts, at 7f (turf) and about 1m (AW; only 7-2), and has no form claims unless she sees the light in first-time blinkers.
runners.comment: Very modest form in first two starts; no form claims without a big turnaround for blinkers

runners.name: Concealed Passion
runners.spotlight: 130,000gns yearling; seventh foal; by Night Of Thunder; winning siblings include King Of Conquest (including three Listed wins at 1m2f-1m4f; RPR 117) and Lady Boba (1m2f/1m4f including Group 3; 109); dam 1m2f and 10.5f (Listed) winner; has a solid standard to reach but market can guide.
runners.comment: Well-connected newcomer from respected yard; solid standard to aim at; needs market check

runners.name: Withtearsinmyeyes
runners.spotlight: 65,000gns yearling; fourth foal; by Invincible Spirit; winning siblings include 7f/1m winner Sindria (RPR 94) and Spanish 8.7f winner Chelsea Square; dam placed 1m4f (RPR 74); probably faces tough start to career.
runners.comment: 65,000gns yearling; probably faces a tough start to her career

runners.name: Tiny Tiger
runners.spotlight: Has achieved very little in two 1m starts on AW this year; looks set for a modest handicap mark following this race.
runners.comment: Has achieved very little in two 1m starts on AW; looks set for modest handicap mark

runners.name: Tashi Delek
runners.spotlight: 28-1 and nothing doing on her Newmarket debut but she revealed more in a first-time eyeshield when runner-up to a next-time novice winner, at Yarmouth (also 7f; soft) in September; now hooded and has raced freely so the extra furlong raises a query; handicaps may offer more scope.
runners.comment: Nothing doing on debut; better when 2nd over 7f in September; races freely so trip query

runners.name: Echo Valley
runners.spotlight: An outsider when well held in two AW maidens (1m3f and about 1m1f) at the start of the year; handicaps will be more her line.
runners.comment: Well held in two AW maidens (1m3f and about 1m1f) early in the year; handicaps soon

runners.name: Cortado Girl
