runners.spotlight: 125-1 for Lingfield AW debut over 7f in September when never recovering from a slow start; one to watch for now.
runners.comment: 125-1 for Lingfield AW debut over 7f in September when never recovering from a slow start

races.race_name: Racing TV Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Hen Party
runners.spotlight: Four good runs since dropped to 6f, on turf and AW; she hasn't shifted much in form terms in that time but it's possible the return to turf will suit; ought to be on the premises.
runners.comment: Four good runs over 6f; hasn't shifted much in form terms but return to turf could suit

runners.name: Egotistical
runners.spotlight: Promising in two starts, doing well despite the bit slipping through her mouth on sole 2yo start, at Southwell (6f) in December and coming home strongly when runner-up to odds-on winner at Newcastle (5f) in January; return to 6f looks sure to suit on turf debut; major chance.
runners.comment: Shown plenty in two AW starts at 6f and 5f; return to 6f looks sure to suit on turf debut

runners.name: Mehmas Engine
runners.spotlight: Turned over when odds-on for 5f debut on Southwell AW and no serious inroads on the first two when upped to 6f at Kempton recently; looks capable of better on turf debut and not ruled out.
runners.comment: Beaten when odds-on for 5f AW debut; fair 3rd upped to 6f; capable of better on turf debut

runners.name: Elshie
runners.spotlight: 52,000euros yearling; third foal; by Blue Point; half-sister to winners Curran (1m2f-1m6f; RPR 86) and Professor Longhair (1m AW; 85); dam Group-placed 1m4f winner (99); dam's side of pedigree suggests longer trips will be in order but sire could inject speed.
runners.comment: Dam's side of pedigree suggests longer trips will suit but sire could inject speed

runners.name: Frantic
runners.spotlight: Caught napping for pace on Leicester debut (6f, soft) in October but she rallied and only just failed to get up; failed to flourish over 7f on Tapeta in November but the return to these conditions could pay dividends.
runners.comment: Came home strongly when only just failed on 6f turf debut; not so good over 7f on AW after

runners.name: Unique Procedure
runners.spotlight: 15,000gns foal; third foal; by Zoustar; winning siblings include Kendall Roy, multiple 5f winner in Britain and elsewhere (99) and Hippocratic Oath (about 1m; 76), dam 5f/6f winner (92); hood fitted on debut so may be one to watch this time.
runners.comment: Bred to be speedy but hood fitted on debut and may be one to watch this time

runners.name: Sweet Desire
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam ran well on debut for yard, then won over 1m next time (RPR 82), winning siblings include useful 7f-1m winner Hay Dude (also Group 3-placed); others likely to have the legs of her on belated debut.
runners.comment: Nice enough pedigree but others likely to have the legs of her over 6f on belated debut

runners.name: Always Perfect
runners.spotlight: 77,000euros breeze-up buy; bred to sprint but her fourth of 11 on Newcastle AW debut (7f; tongue tied; 28-1) was a remote one; this trip may well suit better but she has some work to do.
runners.comment: Bred to sprint so may fare better over 6f than on her recent 7f debut when a remote fourth

runners.name: Bintaryana
runners.spotlight: Smart sprinting pedigree, winning siblings include Group 3/Listed winner Diligent Harry and Harry Three; 33-1 for 6f debut at Haydock in October where she raced freely but hinted at ability against rivals with experience; open to improvement and a likely contender.
runners.comment: Smart sprinting pedigree; 33-1 for Haydock debut in October but showed ability; contender

runners.name: Nzuri
runners.spotlight: 7-1 for Lingfield AW debut (6f) in September but she finished way back after starting slowly; SP that day suggested she wasn't a no-hoper and interesting to see how she goes in the market this time.
runners.comment: 7-1 for AW debut (6f); finished way back but presumably had shown something at home

races.race_name: Burger King Flamin Champion Handicap

runners.name: Faithful Dream
runners.spotlight: 0-11 but has been running with credit on AW this year, meeting trouble in running on several occasions; sports first-time cheekpieces and drops down in trip on this return to turf; should be thereabouts.
runners.comment: 0-11 but has been running with credit on AW this year; drops back in trip.

runners.name: Rosieisme Darling
runners.spotlight: Has been busy on AW during the winter, winning at Lingfield (5f) in February; needs to reproduce that form on turf and if she does so then is another player.
runners.comment: Has been busy on AW during the winter, winning at Lingfield (5f) in February.

runners.name: Magnificent Mel
runners.spotlight: Finished last on two turf starts for Richard Hannon last July; inconsistent in seven AW starts for current trainer over 6f/7f since; drops back in trip and needs to prove her effectiveness on turf.
runners.comment: Inconsistent in seven AW starts for current trainer over 6f/7f.

runners.name: Doilookokay
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark in a Chelmsford nursery on AW last August but has failed to build on that since; sports first-time cheekpieces on this return to turf; needs a revival.
runners.comment: Won a Chelmsford nursery on AW last August but has failed to build on that since.

runners.name: Amakhala
runners.spotlight: Has finished last in all bar one of his six starts with first-time cheekpieces (retained) having seemingly little effect last time; drops back in trip on this return to turf and massive improvement is required.
runners.comment: Has finished last in all bar one of his six starts.

runners.name: Hellcat
runners.spotlight: Third in a couple of 5f maidens at Thirsk last spring but showed little subsequently stepped up to 6f at York and on her nursery debut back at Thirsk last September; sold to join new yard for £1,800 in January and needs to rediscover her early form back in trip.
runners.comment: Third in a couple of 5f maidens at Thirsk last spring but ran moderately subsequently.

runners.name: Staniel Cay
runners.spotlight: Consistent in his four runs so far and may well progress for the step back in trip after disputing the lead until weakening into fourth on his handicap debut at Kempton (6f, AW) last month; 2lb lower and should not be far away.
runners.comment: Disputed the lead until weakening into fourth on his handicap debut at Kempton last month.

runners.name: Mereside Spark
runners.spotlight: Stayed on late when a good second of nine in 5f Beverley novice in September; dwelt at the start when last of six on her AW/handicap debut at Newcastle (5f) in January and needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: Second in 5f Beverley novice in September but finished last at Newcastle in January.

runners.name: Maid In Devon
runners.spotlight: Showed little in three runs on turf last year but caught the eye when keeping on well to finish fourth of eight on her AW/handicap debut at Wolverhampton (6f) last month; should run her race if she can reproduce that effort down in trip and back on turf.
runners.comment: Kept on well to finish fourth on her AW/handicap debut at Wolverhampton last month.

runners.name: Independent Angel
runners.spotlight: Sent off at big prices and has run to a low level in seven starts; hard to recommend.
runners.comment: Sent off at big prices and has run to a low level in seven starts; hard to recommend.

runners.name: Seven Of Hearts
runners.spotlight: Just modest in three runs on AW this year but it would be no surprise if she shows more on her turf/handicap debut for top trainer.
runners.comment: Just modest in three runs on AW this year but may show more on turf/handicap debut.

runners.name: Yesnia
runners.spotlight: Always behind in three AW maidens at Dundalk over 6f/7f last autumn for Ross O'Sullivan; sports a first-time tongue tie and drops in trip on her turf/stable/handicap debut.
runners.comment: Always behind in three AW maidens at Dundalk over 6f/7f last autumn for Ross O'Sullivan.

runners.name: No Claims Bonus
runners.spotlight: Promising third on her handicap debut at Wolverhampton (5f, AW) last September but didn't replicate that in a similar race in November; still early days on this return to turf and may have more to give.
runners.comment: Promising third at Wolverhampton last September but didn't replicate that subsequently.

runners.name: Takincareofbizness
runners.spotlight: Well beaten on his three starts on AW last year; has been gelded since and drops in trip on turf/handicap debut; market can guide.
runners.comment: Well beaten on his three starts on AW last year; drops in trip on turf/handicap debut.

runners.name: Our Hero Matty
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden whose form after being gelded is inferior to his efforts last spring; headgear worn on last two starts in September is removed and now has questions to answer.
runners.comment: Nine-race maiden whose form after being gelded is inferior to his efforts last spring.

runners.name: Muddy Nora
runners.spotlight: Showed little at massive odds in her three starts last year; drops back in trip on handicap debut but yard still waiting for their first turf winner this term.
runners.comment: Showed little at massive odds in her three starts last year; handicap debut.

runners.name: I'm Dan Dare
runners.spotlight: Moderate form in three runs last August/September; has been gelded since and makes handicap debut; yard yet to get off the mark this term.
runners.comment: Moderate form in three runs last August/September; handicap debut.

races.race_name: Burger King Winning Whopper Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Initial Blue
runners.spotlight: In good form on AW since blinkered; did well to overcome a wide stall and make all at Kempton (6f) ten days ago and could take a bit of pegging pack if as effective back on turf.
runners.comment: Could still be on good mark if he can translate recent AW form back to turf

runners.name: Papa Don't Preach
runners.spotlight: Led close home to score at Wolverhampton just before Christmas but the handicapper has been winning the argument since; a 0-28 turf record is enough reason to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Four AW wins (latest in December) but he's 0-28 on turf

runners.name: Level Up
runners.spotlight: Made a good return to turf when narrowly denied over 5f at Bath (good) 13 days ago; nudged back up 3lb but remains 1lb below the mark he defied on AW last autumn; needs considering.
runners.comment: Went close back on turf latest and remains below last winning mark

runners.name: Scent Of May
runners.spotlight: Turned over a 1-12 shot in Chepstow maiden (5f, good) last August; never involved in an AW handicap in the autumn but retains unexposed potential on return having had only five starts.
runners.comment: 5f maiden win last summer; well held on AW handicap debut in autumn but still early days

runners.name: Jeany May
runners.spotlight: Hard to draw any obvious positives from her Southwell reappearance 16 days ago, taking her record to 1-18; first-time cheekpieces need to make a difference.
runners.comment: Well held on AW reappearance and first-time cheekpieces need to make a difference

runners.name: Winchurch
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on his 2025 best and he hasn't always enjoyed much luck on AW this winter; creditable third of nine at Southwell (6f) back from a short break 23 days ago; Mick Appleby has won this twice in recent years.
runners.comment: Creditable third on AW latest; respected for yard with recent winning form in this race
runners.owner: SBK Racing & B & J Parr Racing Ltd

runners.name: Miss Brazen
runners.spotlight: Out of sorts when last seen in the autumn but has dipped to 1lb below the mark she defied at Thirsk (5f, good) last summer; well handicapped if reviving after six months off.
runners.comment: Well handicapped if bouncing back to form after six-month break; betting to guide

runners.name: High Opinion
runners.spotlight: More miss than hit in 2025 but back on a good mark as a result and a record of two wins and two seconds from his five outings over this C&D means a revival can't be ruled out back from four months off.
runners.comment: Good C&D record and well treated if back to form after four months off

races.race_name: Trot To Burger King Lady Bay Handicap

runners.name: Moonjid
runners.spotlight: Came good at the fourth time of asking for current connections over C&D (good) last autumn; a little below that level on AW in recent weeks but may fare better back on turf; first-time cheekpieces are fitted after hanging left last time.
runners.comment: Below par on AW recently but not discounted back at scene of win last year; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Eagle Day
runners.spotlight: Back to form a little out of the blue when awarded a handicap over the extended 1m at Wolverhampton four weeks ago; retains plenty of handicapping scope on peak form but has done the majority of his winning on AW.
runners.comment: Remains on a good mark after Wolverhampton win but does most of winning on AW

runners.name: Kokanee
runners.spotlight: Winner over 1m2f at Beverley (good) last spring and went agonisingly close to a second career success on Pontefract reappearance (1m, good to soft) 11 days ago; a 2lb nudge shouldn't prevent another prominent showing if in similar form.
runners.comment: Close second on recent 1m Pontefract return; prominent showing likely if in same form

runners.name: Commander Of Life
runners.spotlight: Consistent in defeat on AW this year, reaching the frame for the sixth outing in succession when fourth of 13 at Southwell (1m) 11 days ago; turf winner last year; likely to be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Consistent in defeat on AW this year and should be thereabouts back on turf

runners.name: Highland Olly
runners.spotlight: C&D winner on good last spring and added to tally when easily making all at Musselburgh (7f, good) last August; unplaced in four runs since but led for a long way when fifth on 7f Wolverhampton reappearance 18 days ago and could be spot on now.
runners.comment: C&D win last May; led for long way when fifth on AW return and could be spot on now

runners.name: Pulsar Star
runners.spotlight: Second on good turf for James Tate last autumn and has bettered that form when reaching the frame in AW novices over 1m and 7f for new yard; unexposed handicap newcomer.
runners.comment: Promise in novice events and unexposed now handicapping

runners.name: Influential
runners.spotlight: All three starts over 1m, showing promise on the first of two turf runs; no match for a smart prospect when second of three on Newcastle's Tapeta when last seen in October but showed enough to confirm he possesses a fairly useful level of ability; has obvious unexposed potential now handicapping on his return (has been gelded).
runners.comment: Promise last year and makes obvious appeal as an unexposed sort from a leading stable

runners.name: Oceans Five
runners.spotlight: Returned from a long absence to finish third in two AW novices this winter but made a disappointing start to his handicap career when 12l fifth at Chelmsford (1m) 37 days ago.
runners.comment: Third in two AW novices this winter but disappointing start to handicap career latest

runners.name: Pension Pot
runners.spotlight: Showed a fair level of ability in three qualifying runs over 6f/7f last autumn; hasn't obviously shaped as if crying out for this step up in trip but still needs monitoring in the betting on this handicap debut and reappearance.
runners.comment: Fair form in three qualifying runs last autumn; one to note in betting on handicap debut

races.race_name: £9 Racedays At Nottingham Racecourse Handicap

runners.name: Law Supreme
runners.spotlight: Made it 4-7 since cheekpieces went on when seeing off ten rivals back on turf at Bath (1m, good) 13 days ago; a 3lb rise shouldn't prevent another prominent showing in his current mood.
runners.comment: Made it 4-7 since cheekpieces went on when seeing off ten rivals at Bath recently; player

runners.name: Show Biz Kid
runners.spotlight: A bit below form on his final five starts last year but a revival can't be ruled out on return having dipped to 3lb below the mark he defied at Yarmouth (1m2f, good) last April.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 out of sorts but well treated if a break has revived

runners.name: Star Start
runners.spotlight: In good form with a pair of 1m2f fast-ground wins last summer; ought to be sharper for Doncaster reappearance 13 days ago and on a workable mark if back close to his best.
runners.comment: Should be sharper for reappearance and feasibly treated if back close to his best

runners.name: Wicklow Way
runners.spotlight: Improved effort back from five months off when opening his account in a 1m2f Newcastle classified event 37 days ago, his first attempt at this trip; did quite well to get up late on having had to wait for a run and could be on a good mark back in a handicap.
runners.comment: Did well to get up on AW reappearance, his first attempt at 1m2f; could be on good mark

runners.name: Joseph
runners.spotlight: Overcame major early setback to break through at 16th time of asking in apprentice race at Lingfield (1m2f) in February; backed that up when second of seven over 1m3f at Kempton last month; down the field on both previous turf starts but bit too soon to suggest he won't prove as good on the grass.
runners.comment: Claims if he can translate good recent AW form to turf (well held both previous attempts)

runners.name: In The Post
runners.spotlight: Weakened into sixth behind Law Supreme at Bath (1m, good) 13 days ago but he's entitled to come on for that first outing in six months; cheekpieces are added now; remains unexposed after five starts but stamina seemed stretched on previous attempt at 1m2f.
runners.comment: May strip fitter for return but weakened on previous attempt at 1m2f; cheekpieces go on

runners.name: Bearwith
runners.spotlight: Got his head back in front at Newcastle (1m2f) in February; down the field both starts since, although a wide trip didn't help back on turf at Doncaster three weeks ago; hasn't had much luck with the draw again.
runners.comment: AW win in February; held twice since but was caught wide back on turf latest

runners.name: Boubyan
runners.spotlight: Got his head back in front off this mark on AW at Newcastle (1m2f) last month; not beaten far back there next time and this is a bit easier than the Class 4 he contested at Wolverhampton latest.
runners.comment: This is easier than race he ran in last time and he won off this mark on AW last month

runners.name: Bay Dream Believer
runners.spotlight: Reeled off a fast-ground hat-trick over this trip last August; also runner-up off 2lb higher at Redcar (1m2f, good to soft) in the autumn but never involved on Doncaster return three weeks ago.
runners.comment: Three 1m2f wins last August but never involved on Doncaster return three weeks ago

runners.name: Volto Di Medusa
runners.spotlight: Dual 1m winner (good turf/AW); shaped quite well on AW first two starts this year but blotted his copybook when losing many lengths at the start back on turf at Thirsk last week; more than capable off this mark but not one for maximum faith after that.
runners.comment: Capable off this mark but will need to break a lot better than he did at Thirsk last week

runners.name: Caph Star
runners.spotlight: Useful form when winning AW novice for Roger Varian in August 2022; unlikely he retains close to that level of ability but latest Kempton fifth (beaten under 3l) was his best effort since returning from an absence and being eased another 2lb can only help now back on the grass.
runners.comment: Best effort back from long absence when fifth on AW latest; needs to build on that now

runners.name: Wood Whisperer
runners.spotlight: Travelled with purpose close to the pace before fading late into fifth over 1m3f at Southwell 16 days ago; today's slightly shorter trip should help and retains potential after only six starts.
runners.comment: Travelled well for a long way over 1m3f on AW latest; remains unexposed after six starts

runners.name: Dream Pirate
runners.spotlight: Back to winning ways on Lingfield's AW (1m2f) in January; his four successes prior to that all came on turf (including 1m here) so he should remain competitive off only 2lb higher if fully primed after three months off.
runners.comment: AW win in January; this course scorer should figure if fully primed for first run since

runners.name: Onslow Gardens
runners.spotlight: Might have been a shade unlucky when close second over 1m here last August and he was a respectable third over 1m2f at Redcar (good to soft) when last seen in the autumn; should be competitive if ready to roll after 196 days off
runners.comment: Went close off 2lb higher here last summer; claims if ready to roll after 196 days off
runners.owner: Dr M Voikhansky & Mrs S Voikhanskaya

runners.name: Kasgani
runners.spotlight: 0-5 in maidens at up to 1m4f in Ireland for Dermot Weld; sold for 16,000euros in November; remains unexposed but the betting is perhaps the best guide to expectations on handicap and yard debut after seven months off; drawn widest.
runners.comment: A new recruit for Declan Carroll; the betting should help guide to expectations

races.race_name: Ladies Day 9th May Handicap (Div I)

runners.name: Back From Dubai
runners.spotlight: Nine-time winner, with seven on Tapeta and two on turf; finished last of eight after a slow start on Southwell reappearance (1m) last month, but he's well handicapped on his best form last year including when runner-up in this race; needs a close look back in this sphere and he won't mind what the weather does.
runners.comment: Nine-time winner who is well treated on his second in this race last year; interesting

runners.name: Tilsworth Max
runners.spotlight: Just one win from 27 starts but that came at Lingfield (7f) in January and he's finished a close third there in last two starts; remains feasibly treated off same mark as last time but he still has something to prove on turf and his best form has been at 7f.
runners.comment: In good form on AW but he still has something to prove on turf and 7f is his best trip

runners.name: Sceptic
runners.spotlight: 6yo who turned things around with two creditable efforts at Wolverhampton (8.6f) last month; his last two wins have been at that track but he remains well treated on his old turf form; could be dangerous back in this sphere but he probably wouldn't want any rain.
runners.comment: Back on track with creditable efforts at Wolverhampton last month and he's in the mix

runners.name: Fifty Sent
runners.spotlight: Returned to form when winning two classified events at Southwell (1m) last month but he flopped in his hat-trick bid there (handicap) last time; has bit to prove again after that heavy defeat and he's 1-17 on turf.
runners.comment: Flopped in his bid for a Southwell hat-trick last month and he's just 1-17 on turf

runners.name: Al Baahy
runners.spotlight: Won two classified events on AW in January but he's been well below form in handicaps in his last two runs; this trip is no problem but his sole turf win was almost three years ago and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Well below form on AW in last two starts and sole turf win was almost three years ago

runners.name: Penny Ghent
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent 24-race maiden; went close at a big price in a Redcar handicap (1m, good) in November but she ended last year with a tame effort at Wolverhampton; on dangerous mark but she has a modest record when fresh and others are more convincing.
runners.comment: On dangerous mark but she's 0-24 and comes with risks attached on her return

runners.name: Bizarre Law
runners.spotlight: Only one win since June 2022 and has beaten just two rivals in his last five starts (either side of wind surgery); mark has bottomed out now and he has a lot to prove; cheekpieces back on.
runners.comment: Only one win since 2022 and he's struggled in his last five starts; opposable

runners.name: Molly Marine
runners.spotlight: Has worn a hood in all four starts; finished down the field at big prices in her qualifying runs and was a remote last of nine on handicap debut at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) last month; still early days but she needs a transformation at this new trip.
runners.comment: Unexposed 4yo but she needs a transformation at this new trip

runners.name: Freedom Bay
runners.spotlight: 11-race maiden who was beaten 10l or more in her final three runs for Stuart Kittow last season; had wind surgery but she needs a major revival on return for new trainer.
runners.comment: 11-race maiden and she needs a major revival on return for new trainer

runners.name: Evelyn's Phoenix
runners.spotlight: Three wins (7f-1m2f) in 2024 but it was a struggle last year and he was beaten 13l at Redcar in his final run in October; has tumbled down the weights but he needs to turn things around after 173 days off.
runners.comment: Well treated on his old form but it was a struggle last season; lots to prove on return

races.race_name: Ladies Day 9th May Handicap (Div II)

runners.name: Law Degree
runners.spotlight: Two soft-ground wins (7.4f/1m) in 2024 but he's drawn a blank since and has been well held on the Flat and over hurdles in his last three starts; bit to prove and he needs a big turnaround back in this sphere; cheekpieces reapplied.
runners.comment: On long losing run and he needs a big turnaround back on the Flat

runners.name: Desiderata
runners.spotlight: Not the easiest and plenty of short-priced defeats to his name but he won at Wolverhampton (8.6f) last month and was a creditable third in another classified event at Lingfield (1m2f) three weeks ago; remains unexposed on turf and he needs a close look back in this sphere.
runners.comment: Won a classified event at Wolverhampton (8.6f) last month; unexposed on turf

runners.name: James Park Woods
runners.spotlight: Well held in his last four starts but he may have needed the run at Bath two weeks ago and he's now back on the same mark as when completing a double at Brighton (1m, soft) in September; has claims if he can recapture that form and any rain would be a bonus.
runners.comment: Well held in last four runs but his last win was off this mark; needs checking in market

runners.name: Clipsham Noble
runners.spotlight: Won at Brighton (1m, good to firm) for Alice Haynes last June but he's been below form in last seven runs including on stable debut at Southwell last month; overall record is now 1-24 and others are more convincing; cheekpieces reapplied.
runners.comment: Overall record of 1-24 and he's been below form in his last seven runs; others preferred

runners.name: United Force
runners.spotlight: Three wins (1m) in 2024 but he's been well held in last ten starts including when fourth behind Desiderata at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW) last month; that wasn't bad but he needs to raise his game back in a handicap.
runners.comment: Three wins in 2024 but he's been well held in last ten starts; needs to raise his game

runners.name: Mooretown Lad
runners.spotlight: Sole win was in Ireland in 2024 but he's on a reduced mark and has shown some encouraging signs at Wolverhampton (8.6f/9.4f) this spring; usually comes from off the pace and could be dangerous if he gets the breaks.
runners.comment: On reduced mark and could be dangerous if this sets up for his closing style

runners.name: Haaf A Diamond
runners.spotlight: Returned to form with a good fourth at Lingfield (1m, AW) in September but she was back in the doldrums in her final run last season; record is now 1-21 and she finished down the field on her reappearance last spring; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Record of 1-21 and was down the field on her reappearance last spring; headgear on
runners.owner: Dr M Voikhansky & Mrs S Voikhanskaya

runners.name: Charencey
runners.spotlight: His last two wins were over 7f in 2024 but he's well treated on his old form and he ran well on a few occasions at up to 1m2f last season; seems to handle most ground and he needs watching in market after six months off; cheekpieces removed.
runners.comment: Well treated on his old form and he needs watching in market after six months off

runners.name: Raysham
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden but he ended last season with placed efforts in 1m2f handicaps at Pontefract and Redcar (both on good to soft); tongue-tie is removed on this drop back in trip but he's on same mark as last time and that was a personal best; interesting contender on his return.
runners.comment: 0-7 but he ended last season with two placed efforts in handicaps; interesting on return

runners.name: Love You More
runners.spotlight: Creditable third in a Leicester handicap (7f, good to soft) in September but she's not gone on from that and was laboured at Chelmsford in her final run last year; record is now 0-9 and she needs a major revival after a break; hood reapplied.
runners.comment: Nine-race maiden who finished down the field in final three runs last year; down the list

races.race_name: Moet & Chandon Champagne Stakes (Group 1) (Turf)

runners.name: Campione D'Italia
runners.spotlight: Shooting for hat-trick this venue. Showed plenty of fight to secure victory this venue. Breaks from a low barrier. More ground a plus.
runners.owner: Newgate Bloodstock, China Horse Club Racing Et Al

runners.name: Grinzinger Heart
runners.spotlight: Let-up (42-days). Led, held on desperately to win at Flemington 7 Mar. Since trialled at Wagga Riverside 8 Apr. Should relish the rise in distance.
runners.owner: J R Wheeler, P E Brooksbank Et Al

runners.name: Zambales
runners.spotlight: Sent out $11 when laboured to finish 5th in rain affected going this track. Good draw. Unexposed at trip. A few runs under his belt this campaign and can make his presence felt.

runners.name: Miss Chanel
runners.spotlight: Got back and hit line with a full head of steam here. Added journey should assist. Well into preparation now and among the dangers if things pan out.
runners.owner: A G Harvey, Yu Long Investments Et Al

runners.name: Fireball
runners.spotlight: Won the Inglis Millennium (RL) Randwick 7 Feb. Fought bravely to the wire in the Inglis Sires' this venue. Jumps from better barrier. Added journey should assist.
runners.owner: James Harron Bloodstock Colts, TFI Et Al

runners.name: Salann
runners.spotlight: On a quick back-up. Out the back, closed with raking strides in the Fernhill Mile here at her second race start. Good gate. Can fill the minors here.
runners.owner: Affinity Thoroughbred Racing, Silks And Sashes Et Al

runners.name: Diameter
runners.spotlight: On a quick back-up. Scrubbed up from the wide gate and boxed on bravely for a 0.9 len win this track. Could add some value to exotics.

runners.name: Southend
runners.spotlight: Delivered a steely performance to score Drinkwise Baillieu at Rosehill in only his second race appearance. Others carry more punch.
runners.owner: Glentree Racing, J B Wilson Et Al

runners.name: Persian Wonder
runners.spotlight: Handy, knuckled down with purpose in soft going at Rosehill when in the market. Has a tricky draw. Has his first look at 1600m here. Goes into wider exotics.
runners.owner: W M Shelton, Mrs S E Shelton Et Al

runners.name: Nomadic
runners.spotlight: Ridden in back half, strong late to fill the minors in soft going Drinkwise Baillieu at Rosehill last start. Very hard to enthuse.
runners.owner: M J Grace, M T Cullen Et Al

runners.name: Sumac
runners.spotlight: On a quick back-up. Sent out $10 when never got into the race this track over this trip. Others more persuasive.
runners.owner: Mrs C M Cook, Mrs J B Cook Et Al

runners.name: Don't Look Back
runners.spotlight: On a quick back-up. Off pace, worked his way into the minors without threatening winner this venue 11 Apr. Has an adverse draw. Looking elsewhere.
runners.owner: P Perry, Ms C G Collis Et Al

runners.name: Am I Dreaming
runners.spotlight: On a quick back-up. Ran on fairly from back half this venue over this distance. Strikes a harder race.
runners.owner: J N Pilkington, Ms G S Cam Et Al

races.race_name: Oaklawn Handicap (Grade 2) (Dirt)

runners.name: Sovereignty
runners.spotlight: Improved throughout 3yo season in 2025 and crowned US Horse of the Year; winner of Grade 1 Kentucky Derby at Churchill Downs in May (beat Journalism by one and a half lengths) and three times at Saratoga afterwards, notably Grade 1 Belmont Stakes in June (beat Journalism by 3l) and Grade 1 Travers Stakes in August (most impressively by 10l); missed Breeders' Cup Classic with raised temperature; better suited by 1m2f than 1m1f; first start for eight months and must give weight all round on Oaklawn debut.
runners.comment: US Horse of the Year in 2025 when won three 1m2f Grade 1s; gives weight to all on comeback

runners.name: Duke Of Duval
runners.spotlight: Progressive at lower level in 2025, winner of maiden and allowance race at Ellis Park and another allowance race here (1m) last December; unable to make an impact in two Grade 3s this year, on latest start beaten 6l when fifth to Skippylongstocking in Essex Handicap here; needs to spring a massive surprise.
runners.comment: Progressive at lower level last year but well held in two Grade 3s since; very stiff task

runners.name: White Abarrio
runners.spotlight: High-class form at best, winning four Grade 1s including 2023 Breeders' Cup Classic at Santa Anita and 2025 Pegasus World Cup (1m1f) at Gulfstream Park; not quite so good last four starts, though shaped as if in need of race (clear 1f out, tired) when second to stablemate Skippylongstocking in 2026 Pegasus World Cup in January; can race up with the pace; Oaklawn debut.
runners.comment: Winner of four Grade 1s, but again not at his best when 2nd in Grade 1 Pegasus in January
runners.owner: C2 Racing Stable LLC, Gary Barber & La Milagrosa Stable LLC

runners.name: Publisher
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden after his first 12 starts (beaten over 30l by Sovereignty in 2025 Kentucky Derby) but seems improved as 4yo; winner of all three starts over 8.5f here in 2026, namely maiden, allowance and black-type stakes, the latter won by just over 1l, staying on strongly; needs to improve markedly to trouble these, but at least he's going the right way and should be suited by 1m1f.
runners.comment: Improved in 2026, winning three times here over 8.5f including black-type race; stiff task
runners.owner: Gus King & The Estate Of Brereton C Jones

runners.name: Journalism
runners.spotlight: Won three Grade 1s in 2025, including Preakness Stakes at Pimlico (1m1.5f) in May and Haskell Stakes at Monmouth Park (1m1f) in July; good efforts when in frame in similar events afterwards, on final start just under 4l fourth in Breeders' Cup Classic at Del Mar; second over 1m2f to Sovereignty in Kentucky Derby (beaten just over 1l) and Belmont Stakes (beaten 3l) but now 4lb better off and reverts to best trip; sound claims to reverse that form on Oaklawn debut.
runners.comment: Won three Grade 1s around 1m1f; twice beaten by Sovereignty at 1m2f but now 4lb better off
runners.owner: Bridlewood Farm, Don Alberto Stable Et Al

runners.name: Liberal Arts
runners.spotlight: Very useful form at slightly shorter than this 1m1f (has won five times, notably one Grade 3) but nothing to suggest he can run with this class of rival; best form when chasing good pace; sold $120,000 since latest start.
runners.comment: Very useful form at slightly shorter than this but he's likely to be outclassed

races.race_name: Maiden Race (Turf)
Scotty Brand Handicap Chase (Premier Handicap) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Sans Bruit
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who was 11l third of 15 at Aintree (2m, good to soft) nine days ago when bidding to win that race for the third consecutive year (wins on soft/good); his last five wins have come in the month of April and there can be optimism that he will make another bold bid; third in this in 2024.
runners.comment: Ran well in his favourite Aintree race recently and he can make another bold bid

runners.name: Matata
runners.spotlight: Something to prove regarding current form although he didn't take to the National fences when pulled up mid-race in the Topham at Aintree last week; this is a lower grade than he's used to contesting and he's capable of playing a big role if back on song now returned to conventional fences.
runners.comment: Pulled up mid-race in the Topham but this is a lower grade than he's used to contesting

runners.name: Traprain Law
runners.spotlight: Running well in the main this season, winning at Wetherby (1m7f, soft) in December and good third at Musselburgh (2m4f, good to soft) in January last time; this two-time course winner was runner-up in this race two years ago and could again have a part to play.
runners.comment: Runner-up in this race two years ago and in good heart this term; could have part to play

runners.name: Calico
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in the Grand Annual at Cheltenham (2m, good) last month but didn't run badly in 11th and this race isn't as hot; this 10yo showed career-best form earlier in the season with two high-profile wins (2m/2m1f, good to soft) and it will be no surprise if he's in the shake-up.
runners.comment: Two wins last autumn and mid-division in the Grand Annual last time; this isn't as hot

runners.name: Moudan
runners.spotlight: 7yo who showed improvement to win a handicap chase at Navan (2m1f, soft to heavy) last month and again ran well when 10l second of 12 at Fairyhouse (2m, soft) a fortnight ago behind a well-backed winner; 2lb wrong at the weights today but this Irish challenger could have more to offer and is respected.
runners.comment: In good form in Irish handicaps on his last two starts and he's entitled to respect

runners.name: Palacio
runners.spotlight: He's had his ups and downs in a short chasing career and his jumping can lack fluency but he made it 2-5 in this sphere when making virtually all to win at Newbury (2m, good to soft) four weeks ago; won on soft ground over hurdles; major player if things click again today.
runners.comment: He's had his ups and downs but made it 2-5 over fences when winning at Newbury latest

runners.name: Le Nez Creux
runners.spotlight: 2-4 over fences in France, the wins coming last autumn (2m1f, very soft/heavy); changed hands for 320,000euros in November; her top Irish trainer won this race last year and this lightly raced 5yo is one to be interested in on her stable debut, especially if strong in the betting.
runners.comment: 2-4 over fences in France; acquired for 320,000euros; Irish stable won this race last year

runners.name: Aeros Luck
runners.spotlight: Went close in small field at Musselburgh (2m, good to soft) in February and again ran well when 11l third of eight at Kelso (2m1f, good to soft) last time; however, he's up against it today from 7lb out of the handicap.
runners.comment: Running well in defeat this year but up against it today from 7lb out of the handicap

runners.name: Classic Maestro
runners.spotlight: Good second to progressive novice in handicap at Sandown (1m7f, soft) in January and ran well when 14l third here (2m4f, soft) last month, when weakening late on; 3lb out of the handicap today but not ruled out now back down in trip with a visor replacing cheekpieces; best form on soft/heavy.
runners.comment: 3lb out of the handicap but some good form this season, including here, and not ruled out

races.race_name: Dubai Duty Free Stakes (Registered As The Fred Darling Stakes) (Group 3) (Fillies)

runners.name: Catching The Moon
runners.spotlight: Probably had a bit more in hand than the neck margin suggests in Listed grade at Ayr (6f, soft) to take form figures to 211; leading player on the figures upped further in distance and could well show additional progress to boot.
runners.comment: Listed win at Ayr took record to 211; progressive and has a leading chance on form

runners.name: Spinning Lizzie
runners.spotlight: Fighting fit, having beaten Ellusive Butterfly by a neck at Kempton (1m) on AW/seasonal debut; however, now worse off with that rival and balance of form suggests she's unlikely to follow up in this field.
runners.comment: Fighting fit, having beaten Ellusive Butterfly at Kempton on AW/seasonal debut

runners.name: Princess Petrol
runners.spotlight: Gained her novice win at Ascot (6f) then showed Group form that comprised a close second at Newmarket (7f) and fourth of five at Longchamp (1m); holds a respectable chance on bare ratings in first-time hood.
runners.comment: Gained her novice win at Ascot then showed Group form; rating is respectable

runners.name: Touleen
runners.spotlight: Gained stylish wins over 7f at Newbury (good to firm) and Leicester (good) prior to having an excuse (looked ill at ease on the Newmarket track) when unplaced favourite in the Rockfel, which was won by the Leicester runner-up; big player on ratings as things stand and retains potential to boot; commands respect.
runners.comment: Gained two stylish wins, including over C&D, prior to an excusable defeat in the Rockfel

runners.name: Zooming
runners.spotlight: No match for Splish Splash, who admittedly had the benefit of experience, at Haydock (6f) but looks set for a tighter rematch with that rival having registered a neck win at Newmarket (7f, good to soft) next time and being a filly who could well improve further; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Ties in with Splish Splash on debut form; won at Newmarket next time; one to consider

runners.name: Ellusive Butterfly
runners.spotlight: Has switched to AW this year, making all in 7f maiden at Southwell then neck second to Spinning Lizzie (who received 4lb) in 1m conditions race at Kempton; faces a stiffer task back on turf.
runners.comment: Neck second to Spinning Lizzie at Kempton last time; stiffer task back on turf

runners.name: Sukanya
runners.spotlight: Record of 0-4 since winning at this venue on debut, albeit ran well in Group 3 at Goodwood and Listed event at Leopardstown, both over 7f; returned to Newbury on reappearance, this may be the time to catch her.
runners.comment: Returned to Newbury on reappearance, this may be the time to catch her

runners.name: Blingy's Sister
runners.spotlight: Consistent last term, without managing to add to debut win; placed in sales race at the Curragh (6.3f) and creditable fifth in Group 3 at Newmarket (7f) on last two starts; needs improvement on reappearance.
runners.comment: Consistent last term, without managing to add to debut win; needs improvement

runners.name: Splish Splash
runners.spotlight: Strong 6f form in October when last seen, gaining her comfortable maiden win at the chief expense of a subsequent scorer (Zooming) then running well in Listed grade at Newmarket where she was only one place behind Azleet who landed the Nell Gwyn on Wednesday; worth another crack at 7f and probably has a better chance than bare ratings suggest.
runners.comment: Solid effort on final 2yo start, only one place behind the subsequent Nell Gwyn winner

runners.name: Figjam
runners.spotlight: Quite a nice prospect for Katie Scott in 2026, having performed well in Listed/Group races (6f, soft/heavy) last autumn, but this looks a difficult reappearance assignment; held by Catching The Moon on Ayr running.
runners.comment: Nice prospect for Katie Scott but this looks a difficult reappearance assignment

runners.name: Zaravina
runners.spotlight: Nearly justified favouritism and showed strong form in conditions race at Deauville (7f, soft) in sole 2yo run, having been beaten only narrowly by a fellow British-trained filly who subsequently ran well in the May Hill; interesting with progress on the cards.
runners.comment: Nearly justified favouritism in Deauville contest; interesting with progress likely

runners.name: Domina Ignis
runners.spotlight: 300,000euros breeze-up purchase who, despite taking a good hold, ran on well for an emphatic 5l success at Southwell (6f, AW) in sole 2yo start; the form has received a few boosts; bred to be suited by this extra furlong and may settle better with hood now applied; brings potential.
runners.comment: Form of emphatic win at Southwell has received a few boosts; brings potential

runners.name: K Sarra
runners.spotlight: Related to several winners for her connections, notably Pride Of Arras (Group 2); duly showed a good deal of promise at Southwell (1m, AW) in sole 2yo start, scoring comfortably, and looks open to significant progress; interesting contender, assuming she copes with the drop in trip.
runners.comment: Related to winners for her connections; scored comfortably at Southwell; promising

runners.name: Silenciosa
runners.spotlight: Won with something to spare at Southwell (7f, AW) and looks a fairly useful prospect for Tom Clover; however, needs huge progress to follow up, being bottom of this pack on bare figures.
runners.comment: Won with something to spare at Southwell but needs major progress to follow up

runners.name: Stimulative Trip
runners.spotlight: Recorded a tidy win over C&D (good to firm) in sole 2yo start and looks a promising filly; absent for ten months but looks an intriguing contender kept to this scenario, especially if the market speaks positively.
runners.comment: Recorded a tidy win over C&D in sole 2yo start; promising and intriguing contender

races.race_name: CPMS Novices' Champion Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Diamond Dealer
runners.spotlight: Won his first two chases (2m7f, good/good to soft) and, after failing to stay 4m at Kelso, he was short-headed by I Wish You at Wetherby (3m, soft) before winning back at Kelso (2m5f, good to soft) four weeks ago; clearly on the up for his top trainer, who has won two of the last six runnings.
runners.comment: 3-5 over fences and he's clearly on the up for his top trainer; player

runners.name: Walking On Air
runners.spotlight: Good third off 4lb higher in first-time cheekpieces at Doncaster (3m, soft) in January; however, his form away from Doncaster hasn't been great in the last two seasons and the visor needs to have a positive influence.
runners.comment: Third at Doncaster in January but below par on his next two starts; wears first-time visor

runners.name: Califet En Vol
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten fourth at Kempton (2m4f, good) last month but mistakes didn't help; won there (2m4f, good to soft) in February; he's probably okay on soft ground and his dam won at up to 3m; ran disappointingly at Ayr (2m4f) on Friday.
runners.comment: Won at Kempton in February; ran disappointingly at Ayr (2m4f) on Friday

runners.name: I Wish You
runners.spotlight: Improved form to get off the mark over fences at the third attempt at Wetherby (3m, soft) in February; hit with a 7lb rise but the short-head runner-up Diamond Dealer went one better next time and this 6yo is in good hands to continue to progress; respected.
runners.comment: The form of his Wetherby win was franked and he's in good hands to continue to progress

runners.name: Big John Wayne
runners.spotlight: 8yo who has returned from an absence this season and has improved with each of his three chase starts, winning at Hexham (2m4f, soft) last month; this is a tougher assignment but he did it readily last time and the step up in trip could be a positive move; he's one to consider.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 8yo who won at Hexham and the step up in trip could be a positive move

runners.name: Ayiko
runners.spotlight: He can make mistakes and was pulled up at Fairyhouse just 12 days ago in first-time cheekpieces (retained); however, he jumped to the left and returns to a left-handed track for the first time since his promising second on chase debut at Haydock (2m4f, soft); won 3m maiden hurdle here; interesting.
runners.comment: Pulled up at Fairyhouse just 12 days ago but the return to a left-handed track could help

runners.name: Just Over Land
runners.spotlight: Two chase wins towards the end of last year (2m3f, good to soft) and did well to finish third of five at Sandown (2m4f, soft) last month having made mistakes; pulled up over 3m4f last January on sole previous run beyond 2m5f but he's worth a go at this trip; could go well if finding fluency.
runners.comment: 3rd at Sandown despite mistakes; this trip is worth a go; could go well if finding fluency

runners.name: Al Kalila
runners.spotlight: Course maiden hurdle winner (2m4f, soft) who built upon previous chasing promise with 2l win at Kelso (2m5f, good to soft) in February; there can be optimism that he will be fine now back up in trip but he beat only three rivals last time and further improvement is needed in first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Won at Kelso last time but beat only three rivals and further improvement is needed

runners.name: Milcree
runners.spotlight: Very lightly raced 8yo who returned from a long layoff this year and won his first two chase starts (2m4f/2m5f, soft/heavy; including here); pulled up at Uttoxeter (3m, soft) since and hard to be confident on the back of that, but he bled from the nose and remains of interest on his previous exploits.
runners.comment: Won his first two chase starts; pulled up at Uttoxeter last time but remains of interest

runners.name: Young Jack
runners.spotlight: Runner-up on three of his first four chase starts (2m7f/2m5f, soft/heavy) before winning at Newcastle (2m7f, good to soft) last month; it was a small field last time and he's in a hotter race today, but he did it nicely and could have more to offer in the cheekpieces, which he's worn just twice.
runners.comment: He's enjoyed a progressive first season over fences and did it nicely at Newcastle latest
runners.owner: D & D Armstrong Ltd & L Westwood

runners.name: Gamesters Guy
runners.spotlight: Good form when winning a two-runner novice chase at Doncaster (3m, good to soft) in December on his second run over fences; however, he hasn't kicked on since and needs to raise his game if he's to win this.
runners.comment: Good form when winning two-runner novice chase in December but hasn't kicked on since

runners.name: Blakey Boy
runners.spotlight: Took a sizeable step forward when winning over C&D (soft) in February and he followed up at Kelso (3m2f, good to soft) four weeks ago; he's 5lb higher than last time and is in much more competitive race today, but this progressive 6yo could still have more left in the tank.
runners.comment: Has won his last two; this is tougher but this 6yo may have more left in the tank

runners.name: Caughtinyourtrance
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer; beaten just a neck over hurdles at Haydock (3m, good to soft) in December and ran well in defeat last month in his first two chases (2m7f/2m4f, good to soft); has won on soft; each-way possible.
runners.comment: Ran well in defeat last month in his first two chases and he's an each-way possible

runners.name: Kdeux Saint Fray
runners.spotlight: Won at Cheltenham (2m4f, soft) last November on second chase start and finished third in quality handicap at Kempton (3m, good to soft) in February, despite jumping to the left at that right-handed track; massively inconvenienced by controversial start when down the field at the Cheltenham Festival; retains potential, especially going left-handed; high on the list.
runners.comment: Inconvenienced by the start at Cheltenham; retains potential, especially left-handed

runners.name: Pounding Poet
runners.spotlight: Close third over hurdles at this meeting 12 months ago (3m, good to soft) and won in that sphere at Uttoxeter (2m7f, good) last July; ran pretty well in 5l third at Newbury (2m6f, good) in December when back chasing for the first time since 2023, but this 10yo needs a bigger performance today.
runners.comment: 10yo who ran pretty well when back over fences in December but needs a bigger run today
runners.owner: Hft Limted And P J King And Son

races.race_name: Watership Down Stud Too Darn Hot Greenham Stakes (Group 3) (Colts & Geldings)

runners.name: Zavateri
runners.spotlight: Cracking purchase at 35,000gns as a yearling, and developed into a smart colt during a 2025 season that featured wins in three major 2yo races, notably the National Stakes at the Curragh (7f, yielding) where his gameness was confirmed; handled the July course at Newmarket in the summer but proved all at sea on the Rowley Mile in the Dewhurst, losing his unbeaten record (still ran respectably in fourth place); he's the pick on form and launches 3yo campaign in a race that his trainer's father Fulke won twice.
runners.comment: Landed three major 2yo races prior to respectable effort in the Dewhurst; the form pick

runners.name: Ardisia
runners.spotlight: Bargain buy at £33,000 as a yearling, and picked up almost £300k in prize-money during a busy 2025 campaign, including when landing the Two Year Old Trophy at Redcar (6f); ran respectably, amid trouble, upped to 1m at the Breeders' Cup; not certain to progress any further, however.
runners.comment: Money-spinner during a busy 2yo campaign but isn't sure to progress any further

runners.name: Albert Einstein
runners.spotlight: Rare runner in the Greenham for his powerful connections, who are still working out whether the highly regarded colt is a Guineas prospect or needs a return to 6f over which he was 2-2 as a 2yo (both on good ground); only sixth when favourite for Curragh Listed race (7f, soft to heavy; took a good hold) on return from ten-month absence but retains potential and could well take a big step forward, as well as settle better, in first-time hood with the outing under his belt; adds great interest to this field.
runners.comment: Highly regarded colt who looks set to leave his Curragh reappearance effort behind

runners.name: Title Role
runners.spotlight: Has shown further improvement at Meydan this year and landed the Jumeirah 2,000 Guineas (1m, good; Listed) by 1l most recently, taking overall record to 3-5; the form received a boost of sorts at Newmarket this week; sole Newbury effort, namely close second in strong maiden (6f) last May, has worked out very well; solid claims.
runners.comment: Success in the Jumeirah 2,000 Guineas took his record to 3-5; improving colt
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

runners.name: Aqpan
runners.spotlight: Turf debutant who brings a solid AW record that comprises a couple of second-places at Kempton and reappearance success (admittedly faced straightforward task) at Southwell, all over 7f; unexposed but faces a much stiffer assignment upped in class, as ratings reflect.
runners.comment: Brings a solid AW record but faces a much stiffer assignment on turf debut

runners.name: Needle Match
runners.spotlight: Edged ahead inside the final half-furlong and did just enough at Newbury (6.5f, soft) on the same day that Gonna Fly won in better style over 1m; holds a 2,000 Guineas entry, unlike that rival, and is open to progress; bottom of this pack on bare figures as things stand, however.
runners.comment: Won narrowly at Newbury on the same day that Gonna Fly scored in better style

runners.name: Alparslan
runners.spotlight: Comfortably justified favouritism in Curragh sales race (6.3f, yielding) to follow up his debut win; rank outsider in the Dewhurst (7f, good to firm) but ran well on bare figures to finish sixth of nine, just under 2l behind Zavateri; difficult to rule out further improvement this year.
runners.comment: Scored twice before running well, just under 2l behind Zavateri, in the Dewhurst

runners.name: Gonna Fly
runners.spotlight: Scored quite tidily at Newbury (1m, soft) in sole 2yo start; perhaps not crying out for this drop in trip, especially if the ground isn't so slow, and it should be noted that all of his future major entries are over middle distances; otherwise brings potential to Group level.
runners.comment: Quite a tidy success over 1m at Newbury in sole 2yo start; brings potential

races.race_name: Coral Scottish Champion Hurdle (A Limited Handicap) (Grade 2) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Tutti Quanti
runners.spotlight: Won at Newbury (2m, soft) last November on second handicap start and easily followed up there in the prestigious William Hill Hurdle in February (2m, heavy); reluctant to line up and couldn't adopt usual prominent pitch when down the field in the Champion Hurdle at Cheltenham last month; returns to a handicap today and could bounce back with a bold bid.
runners.comment: Won 2 high-profile handicaps then down the field in the Champion Hurdle; could bounce back

runners.name: Tellherthename
runners.spotlight: 7yo who has shown glimpses of significant promise across his light career and didn't enjoy a clear run in the straight when 12th of 23 on his stable debut in the County Hurdle at Cheltenham (2m1f, soft) last month, when beaten only about 8l on his first run since last October; wears a first-time hood and could have plenty more to offer for the Skelton yard.
runners.comment: Caught the eye on stable debut in the County Hurdle and he's firmly in calculations

runners.name: Captain Hugo
runners.spotlight: Progressive novice who made it 3-5 over hurdles when winning the competitive Morebattle Hurdle on handicap debut at Kelso (2m, good to soft) in February; has also shown promise on soft ground; this 6yo may well continue on an upward curve and he's a strong contender up 6lb.
runners.comment: Progressive novice who won the Morebattle Hurdle at Kelso; strong contender up 6lb

runners.name: Secret Squirrel
runners.spotlight: Just two starts this season and down the field in the County Hurdle at Cheltenham last month; however, prior to that he was a creditable third of four in the Grade 2 Kingwell Hurdle at Wincanton (1m7f, heavy) and he was a progressive handicapper last term (various ground); not discounted.
runners.comment: Just two runs this season and down the field in the County Hurdle, but he's not discounted

runners.name: Queenie St Clair
runners.spotlight: She's thrived this season and made it 3-5 for the yard (wins at 1m7f-2m1f on good to soft/heavy) when winning big-field mares' novice handicap at Kelso four weeks ago; from out of the handicap she's 12lb higher today back against males but it remains to be seen where her limitations lie.
runners.comment: Today's mark poses a question but this mare has thrived for a new stable this season

runners.name: Ooh Betty
runners.spotlight: Made all in Grade 2 mares' hurdle at Ascot (1m7f, soft) in January and ran well for a long way when ninth of 23 in the County Hurdle at Cheltenham (2m1f, soft) last month; she's 1lb lower than when a very close third in this last year (good to soft) and could again be involved.
runners.comment: Very close third in this last year; ran well in the County Hurdle at Cheltenham last month

runners.name: Gibbs Island
runners.spotlight: Easily won a competitive 4yo handicap at Chepstow (2m, good) last October on his reappearance; underwhelming on his next three starts but took big step back in right direction when keeping on for seventh of 23 in the County Hurdle at Cheltenham (2m1f, soft) last month; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Big step back in right direction when seventh in the County Hurdle and not ruled out

runners.name: All In You
runners.spotlight: Went close on stable debut in big field at Ascot (1m7f, soft) in December then won at Sandown (2m, good) in January, turning the tables on Dance And Glance who won a nice prize at Haydock this month; 5lb out of the handicap today and pulled up in the William Hill Hurdle at Newbury in February, but very few ran to form in that attritional contest; retains potential; chance.
runners.comment: Made a fine start for this yard and he could leave his Newbury run miles behind

runners.name: Dedicated Hero
runners.spotlight: Following wind surgery he returned to form to win in a small field at Kelso (2m5f, good) recently, and he's fine on slow ground and at this trip; however, from out of the handicap he's 10lb higher today and his profile suggests he'll be vulnerable back up in grade.
runners.comment: Won in small field at Kelso recently; profile suggests he's vulnerable back up in grade

runners.name: Welsh Charger
runners.spotlight: Beaten only 2l when fifth in this last year and runs off just 1lb higher today (from 8lb out of the handicap); however, he hasn't been at his best this season and would be a surprise winner.
runners.comment: 2l fifth in this last year off similar mark but hasn't been at his best this season

races.race_name: Dubai Duty Free Finest Surprise Stakes (Registered As The John Porter Stakes) (Group 3)

runners.name: Al Aasy
runners.spotlight: Held up; eight Group 3 wins are already in the bag, including this race in 2021 (his only appearance in it); has a slightly mixed reappearance record but he was almost as good as ever with his two wins last term, including first time out, and a return to that level would make this 9yo the one to beat.
runners.comment: Eight Group 3 wins, almost as good as ever with his two last term; sets the standard

runners.name: Lion's Pride
runners.spotlight: Not discredited in Qatar in February on latest outing and in the mix judged on peak efforts, including a Newmarket Listed win (1m4f) last September; however, there are also plenty of lesser shows to be considered.
runners.comment: In the mix judged on peak efforts but there are plenty of lesser shows to be considered

runners.name: Tenability
runners.spotlight: Won a novice and three Ascot 1m4f handicaps on varied ground last June-September; creditable second of five in Ascot Group 3 on final outing; none of that form is near what should be required to win this race but he brings potential starting this second season.
runners.comment: Did well last term; his form is not what's required to win this but he brings potential

runners.name: Phantom Flight
runners.spotlight: Four Listed wins before he added a 1m4f Group 3 success in Bahrain 15 days ago; stacks of smart form in his lengthy career and none of it better than that latest start, but a bit extra will be needed to win this.
runners.comment: Four Listed wins before he added a Group 3 success in Bahrain 15 days ago; needs extra

runners.name: Bellum Justum
runners.spotlight: Won Group 2 at Newmarket (1m4f, good) last May in first-time cheekpieces, which were kept for two modest efforts in June but not today; first run since a gelding operation in September; ring-rusty on his 2025 reappearance in this race and the market may reveal whether the same is expected this time.
runners.comment: Ran well only once last term; gelded since; ring-rusty on 2025 reappearance in this race

runners.name: Convergent
runners.spotlight: Pipped in the Deutsches Derby (1m4f) last July before landing a 1m4f Group 3 at Leopardstown and 1m3f Group 2 at Longchamp; flopped on soft ground on final start but he should not face that here and, despite giving weight all round, could be on the premises if resuming on song.
runners.comment: Flopped on soft ground on final start; could be on the premises if resuming on song

races.race_name: Jordan Electrics Ltd Seafield Trophy Mares' Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Twistthenightaway
runners.spotlight: Won on handicap debut at Newcastle (2m4f, heavy) in January and improved again to follow up at Bangor (2m7f, soft); this is a tougher test of her credentials but she's clearly highly progressive for her top yard.
runners.comment: 2-2 in handicaps; this is tougher but she's clearly highly progressive for her top yard
runners.owner: Mrs Paul Shanahan & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Holly Hartingo
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who went close twice in a row at Kempton (3m, good) towards the end of last year following an absence; tailed off at Chepstow since on soft ground she should have handled; needs to bounce back, but not ruled out with first-time blinkers replacing cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Disappointing at Chepstow in February but in very good heart previously and not ruled out

runners.name: Azahara Palace
runners.spotlight: She's run reasonably well, albeit without seriously threatening, in her first two handicap hurdles (2m1f/2m, soft/good to soft); won five times on the Flat (at up to 1m4f) and she could have more to offer in this code, but it remains to be seen whether she'll stay this far.
runners.comment: Respectable efforts in her first two handicap hurdles but those were over 2m1f/2m

runners.name: Princess Keri
runners.spotlight: 6yo who has been a major improver back over hurdles this season, winning three times (2m-2m5f, good-soft); having shot up the weights she again ran well when second of four at Chepstow (2m7f, soft) in February, but she needs to prove her current mark isn't beyond her; 1lb wrong today.
runners.comment: Major improver this season but has to prove her current mark isn't beyond her

runners.name: Game Colours
runners.spotlight: Untested at this trip under rules but there have been hints that she'll stay this far; in good form against fellow mares this winter prior to tailing off in the Imperial Cup at Sandown last month; takes on her own sex today and it will be no surprise to see a bold showing.
runners.comment: Could bounce back with bold showing back against fellow mares; trip may be within range

runners.name: Love Of Neymore
runners.spotlight: Unseated rider at the start at Chepstow two starts ago but she's run some good races this season, winning at Chepstow (2m7f, good to soft) in December and fourth of 15 at Windsor (3m, soft) in January; wears first-time cheekpieces and might not be far away.
runners.comment: She's run some good races this season, including Chepstow win, and might not be far away

runners.name: Fox's Fancy
runners.spotlight: 6l third of nine in this last year (good to soft) and she won here (2m5f, soft) last month; had just three rivals to beat last time but did it nicely from a next-time-out winner; could be in the shake-up.
runners.comment: Third in this last year and won here last month; it was a small field but form franked

runners.name: Starlyte
runners.spotlight: Five-time hurdle winner (2m4f/2m5f, good/good to soft), the latest last March; below best for most of this season and from out of the handicap today she has a career-high mark to defy but she outran her 200-1 odds when staying on strongly for fourth behind World Of Fortunes in a Listed race at Kelso (3m, good to soft) four weeks ago, and perhaps isn't a forlorn hope.
runners.comment: Needs handicap career-best but outran 200-1 odds when Listed fourth at Kelso last month

runners.name: World Of Fortunes
runners.spotlight: Posted her third Listed win in convincing fashion at Kelso (3m, good to soft) four weeks ago; went close in soft-ground Grade 3 the time before; solid credentials back in a handicap.
runners.comment: Posted her third Listed win in convincing fashion at Kelso last month; solid credentials

runners.name: Pinot Rouge
runners.spotlight: Bang there when falling seven out in the Hexham Marathon over fences last month (4m, soft); she's 1lb below her last winning hurdle mark but Hexham seems to bring out the best in her and this inconsistent mare was pulled up in this race 12 months ago; it might be best to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: She's below her last winning mark but Hexham seems to bring out the best in her

races.race_name: Win Unique Experiences At OLBG Prizes Spring Cup Handicap

runners.name: Stem
runners.spotlight: Has run only five times and he signed off for last season with a personal best when second of 15 over C&D (good to soft) in September; has been gelded and makes his reappearance with his potential still intact; one of four runners for Richard Hannon.
runners.comment: Has run only five times; fine second over C&D last autumn; reappears with potential intact

runners.name: Rogue Diplomat
runners.spotlight: Progressive 4yo; 5-6 over 7f but showed he's just as effective at 1m when making Urban Lion pull out all the stops in the Lincoln at Doncaster (good to soft) three weeks ago; 1lb better off for a nose defeat and there should be little between the pair again.
runners.comment: Beaten a nose by Urban Lion in the Lincoln and there should be little between them again

runners.name: Whip Cracker
runners.spotlight: On a 14-race losing sequence but he was runner-up three times last term, including when beaten a head at Glorious Goodwood (1m1f, good); went off third favourite in a big field at the Curragh on reappearance but didn't give his running; on a retrieval mission with Ryan Moore booked.
runners.comment: On 14-race losing run; below par in big field in Ireland on return; Ryan Moore booked

runners.name: Nikovo
runners.spotlight: Kept busy on AW during the winter, winning over 7f at Newcastle and far from disgraced in a valuable 1m event there on Good Friday; however, his turf form is at a lower level and he'll do well to regain the winning thread.
runners.comment: Far from disgraced in valuable AW event last time but his turf form is at a lower level

runners.name: Boyfriend
runners.spotlight: Reliable last season, going close under a positive Sam Hitchcott ride over C&D (good) in May; met some trouble at Newmarket on final start; not discounted, although he'll need a career best from his current mark.
runners.comment: Made most when beaten head here in spring 2025; will need career best from current mark

runners.name: Shout
runners.spotlight: Raced too freely in the Lincoln and ended up shaping nicely in a never-nearer ninth; had ended 2025 on a high with a smooth success at Ascot (1m, soft) and a fine fourth in the Balmoral Handicap (good) over the same C&D; gets on really well with Oisin Murphy and he's back in the saddle today; still unexposed and now looks ready to strike.
runners.comment: Smooth Ascot winner last autumn; too free in Lincoln on return; now looks ready to strike

runners.name: Oliver Show
runners.spotlight: Showed his effectiveness over a straight 1m when beaten a nose in the 2025 Lincoln at Doncaster (good to soft) and he's now 3lb lower; several respectable efforts afterwards, and 6f on good ground was probably too sharp on his sole outing in Bahrain over the winter; certainly well handicapped on best form and well worth a market check after 106 days off.
runners.comment: Had an excuse in Bahrain in January; well handicapped based on last year's Lincoln second

runners.name: Back In Black
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo; consistent last season, winning in cosy fashion at this meeting (7f, good) and going down narrowly when stepped up in trip at Goodwood (1m, good) in the autumn; reappears having been gelded and with his stable in form; interesting contender.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo; close second at Goodwood in the autumn; has been gelded; interesting

runners.name: Fifth Column
runners.spotlight: Below par on his last two 3yo starts but he'd previously won competitive 1m events at Newmarket and York (both good to firm) in the summer, and he did well to prevail at York after having to wait for a run; only 3lb higher here and he'll be a major player if ready to roll after six months off.
runners.comment: Won at Newmarket and York last summer; big player if ready to roll after six months off

runners.name: Classic
runners.spotlight: Chased home Ebt's Guard and Urban Lion in this race last year and he's better off at the weights with both; ran a cracker when dropped to 7f for valuable event at Ascot last summer; hasn't been seen for eight months but he'll be dangerous if cherry ripe.
runners.comment: Third here last year; hasn't been seen for eight months but dangerous if cherry ripe

runners.name: Cogitate
runners.spotlight: Equally effective at 7f and 1m and arrives in good form from the AW; could be thereabouts again, although he finished behind several of these when only seventh in this race 12 months ago.
runners.comment: In good form on AW; finished behind several of these when only seventh 12 months ago

runners.name: Serengeti
runners.spotlight: Has had several trainers in his short career to date; two 7f wins for Aidan O'Brien; not at his best on his last three outings, never dangerous at Kempton on latest (sole run for James Ferguson); probably best watched.
runners.comment: Two 7f wins for Aidan O'Brien but not at best last three outings; another new trainer here

runners.name: Jimmy Speaking
runners.spotlight: Well backed and defeated 15 opponents at Doncaster (7f, good to soft; just as effective over 1m) on reappearance three weeks ago; that was convincing enough but he's up 4lb and in a much stronger race here.
runners.comment: Beat 15 opponents at Doncaster three weeks ago but up 4lb and in a much stronger race here

runners.name: Linwood
runners.spotlight: Has a progressive profile and made it three wins from eight attempts when easily making all at Newmarket (1m, good to soft) last November; subsequently gelded; a 10lb rise means he'll need another personal best but he's a low-mileage 4yo who may have more in the tank.
runners.comment: Progressive; easily made all at Newmarket last November; may have more in the tank

runners.name: Hot Cash
runners.spotlight: Best efforts when ridden up with the pace; form tailed off in 2025 but he's now back to the same mark as when winning at Carlisle (1m, soft; handles good) last summer; gelded since last seen; stablemate of Urban Lion.
runners.comment: Form tailed off in 2025 but back to same mark as when winning at Carlisle last summer

runners.name: Urban Lion
runners.spotlight: Goes well in a big field over a straight 1m; beaten a neck in this race last year (good ground) and made a winning return in the Lincoln at Doncaster (good to soft; beat Rogue Diplomat a nose) three weeks ago; should be in the thick of things again off a 4lb higher mark.
runners.comment: Beaten a neck last year; won the Lincoln at Doncaster three weeks ago; in the mix again

runners.name: Nizam
runners.spotlight: Like Ozat, he's an ex-French gelding who was acquired by Ian Williams (for 90,000euros) in the autumn; won three times (7.5f/1m) from just nine attempts in France; doesn't have an obvious chance off this mark on British debut but he could still be unexposed.
runners.comment: 3-9 in France; no easy task off this mark on British debut but could be unexposed

runners.name: Ebt's Guard
runners.spotlight: Tough and consistent sort who won this race (good ground) on his reappearance 12 months ago and finished second in the Balmoral Handicap at Ascot in the autumn; starts the new season on a career-high mark but he's impossible to rule out.
runners.comment: Tough and consistent; won this race 12 months ago; 13lb higher but impossible to rule out

runners.name: Old Cock
runners.spotlight: 4-10 for Edward Bethell, winning the Hambleton Handicap at York's Dante meeting (1m, good to firm) last spring; changed hands for 26,000gns last autumn; only 3lb higher than at York and doesn't have many miles on the clock.
runners.comment: Four wins for Edward Bethell; reappears for new yard; doesn't have many miles on the clock

runners.name: Checkandchallenge
runners.spotlight: Pretty consistent in Listed/Group races in 2025 without performing to his very best; losing sequence now stands at 18 and he reappears after seven months off in his first handicap since July 2022; cheekpieces left off and others preferred.
runners.comment: Usually runs in Listed/Group races; not quite at best in 2025; others preferred

runners.name: Samuel Colt
runners.spotlight: Very lightly raced 5yo; Group-placed in 2024 for Aidan O'Brien; has failed to beat a rival in three runs for current stable, always behind after stumbling early on at Sandown (behind Urban Lion) last June; hard to recommend on recent form but he has been dropped a chunk in the weights and gelded, and he now reappears in a hood.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 5yo; well held in 2025 but is thrown in on best form and has been gelded

runners.name: Ozat
runners.spotlight: Previously trained in France where he won four times, the last two coming over 8.5f (AW) and 1m1f (good ground) at Chantilly; changed hands for 75,000euros last autumn; makes his British debut in a warm race.
runners.comment: Previously trained in France where he won four times; makes British debut in a warm race

runners.name: Hand Of God
runners.spotlight: Won the Golden Gates Handicap at Royal Ascot (1m2f, good to firm) in June 2024; seen only three times last season and wasn't at his best, although each run came in a highly competitive race; may need a positive ride to get away with this shorter trip on his reappearance, but the handicapper has given him a chance and he's certainly not ruled out.
runners.comment: 1m2f Royal Ascot winner in 2024; seen only three times last year but not ruled out

runners.name: Al Mubhir
runners.spotlight: Smart at his best for William Haggas, winning a Listed race at Sandown (1m2f, good) in July 2024; campaigned solely in Australia in 2025 and well beaten on his last two starts; potentially well handicapped back in Britain for a new yard after wind surgery, but he carries obvious risks.
runners.comment: Smart at best in Britain; form tailed off in Australia in 2025; has had wind surgery

runners.name: Tony Montana
runners.spotlight: In and out last season for Michael Bell, the high points coming when second in big fields at York (good to firm) in May and the Curragh (good to yielding) in September; all his best efforts have come over 1m2f so today's shorter distance is a concern but it's possible he'll be freshened up by a change of stable (same owner).
runners.comment: Reappearance; best form is over further but he could be freshened up by a change of yard

runners.name: Ancient Rome
runners.spotlight: Mostly well held last year and unplaced on five of his six starts; falling in the weights and dangerous if revived by a winter break, but his losing run goes back to September 2023 and he needs to turn over a new leaf.
runners.comment: Mostly well held last year; falling in the weights but needs to turn over a new leaf

races.race_name: Coral Scottish Grand National Handicap Chase (Premier Handicap) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Isaac Des Obeaux
runners.spotlight: Made a bad mistake on penultimate outing but he won starts either side and latest was the 4m2f Midlands National at Uttoxeter (soft) in which he was always prominent and asserted by 8l on run-in, with Git Maker in third; up 7lb but his only attempt at a marathon trip brought a clear career best.
runners.comment: Asserted by 8l on run-in for 4m2f Midlands National at Uttoxeter (soft) last time; up 7lb
runners.owner: G Mason, Sir A Ferguson, C & G Barber

runners.name: Montregard
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to top-class Protektorat; made three notable steps forward on varied ground over 3m at Ascot this season, including for a good prize on latest start when he led two out for a clearcut win; raised another 4lb but he's a rising force, likely to prove well handicapped if he gets this trip, which is also feasible; on the shortlist.
runners.comment: A rising force, likely to prove well handicapped if he gets this trip, which is feasible

runners.name: King Of Answers
runners.spotlight: Won two small-field handicaps at Kelso (soft/heavy) over the extended 2m7f, easily on penultimate start (made most) despite the odd jumping indiscretion; wore cheekpieces that day and again when an excellent second of 17, coming from out the back, in the NH Chase at Cheltenham (3m6f, good to soft); his jumping can be a bit sticky but he's a prime candidate for 4m.
runners.comment: NH Chase 2nd at Cheltenham; jumping can be a bit sticky but he's a prime candidate for 4m

runners.name: Quebecois
